name: Sentinel-3 SLSTR Level-2 Fire Radiative Power
provider: EUMETSAT / Copernicus
theme_canonical: Fire radiative power
theme_raw: SLSTR FRP
coverage: global land and ocean hotspots
license: EUMETSAT Data Store rights field reports Copernicus
formats: Copernicus Sentinel-3 NRT SLSTR Fire Radiative Power product; hotspot locations and radiative power
access: EUMETSAT Data Store product page and browse API collection metadata
doi_or_citation: EO:EUM:DAT:0417
notes: Refreshed in v107 with canonical EUMETSAT Data Store product URL and API evidence. The Copernicus NRT S3 FRP product identifies the location, and quantifies the radiative power, of any hotspot present on land and ocean Earth surfaces, that radiates a heating signal within a pixel size of 1 km2 All threatening hotspots are identified and characterised within three hours from SLSTR observation sensing time. The current version of the NRT S3 FRP processor is mainly applicable during the night while only a few daytime granules, with non-saturated background (ie, no fires) radiance, are processed at this stage. The NRT S3 FRP product will become operational after a higher level of quality and maturity is reached, including the full processing of granules during daytime, a comprehensive global validation, and positive feedback by experts and users. All Sentinel-3 Near Real Time (NRT) products are available at pick-up point in less than 3h.
desc_zh: Sentinel-3 SLSTR Level-2 Fire Radiative Power 是 Copernicus 近实时火点辐射功率产品，自 2021 年 1 月起由 EUMETSAT Data Store 提供。它识别陆地和海洋表面的热点位置并量化 1 km² 像元内的加热辐射信号，主要面向 3 小时内近实时服务。
desc_en: Sentinel-3 SLSTR Level-2 Fire Radiative Power is the Copernicus near-real-time fire radiative power product available through the EUMETSAT Data Store from January 2021 onward. It identifies hotspot locations over land and ocean and quantifies heating radiative power within 1 km² pixels, primarily for near-real-time delivery within three hours.

openness: open data; free registration may be required

The NRT S3 FRP product will become operational after a higher level of quality and maturity is reached, including the full processing of granules during daytime, a comprehensive global validation, and positive feedback by experts and users. All Sentinel-3 Near Real Time (NRT) products are available at pick-up point in less than 3h.

Spanning 1979 to present and distributed as NetCDF-4 through direct download, THREDDS and AWS, it supports climate-scale Earth energy-budget, cloud/water-vapor longwave modulation and model-evaluation studies.

formats: NetCDF via NOAA NCEI/CLASS and NOAA Open Data cloud buckets; product prefix ABI-L2-VAA in GOES-R ABI Level-2 archives

time_span: GOES-R operational archive from GOES-16 era onward; GOES-19 public bucket coverage reflected in 2025 NOAA/AWS registry transition information
notes: Refreshed in v217 after checking NOAA NCEI C01512, GOES-R product documentation and the AWS Open Data Registry. NCEI describes ABI-L2-VAA as containing two images with pixel values identifying volcanic ash cloud height and volcanic ash mass loading. The ash cloud top height is geopotential height; ash mass loading is volcanic ash mass per unit area, in metric tonnes per square kilometer. Product images are produced on the ABI fixed grid at 2 km resolution for Full Disk coverage and are generated for both daytime and nighttime geolocated source data. Satellite-specific cloud buckets are access routes for the same product family, not separate scientific datasets.
desc_zh: NOAA GOES-R ABI Level 2 VAA 产品提供火山灰云顶高度和火山灰质量载荷两个核心图像层，用于航空安全、火山灾害监测和扩散模式初始化。v217 补充确认了 NCEI C01512、GOES-R 产品页和 NOAA AWS 开放数据路径，并明确该产品为 2 km ABI 固定网格 Full Disk 数据，GOES-16/17/18/19 云桶是访问路线而非独立数据集。
desc_en: The NOAA GOES-R ABI Level 2 VAA product provides volcanic ash cloud-top height and ash mass-loading image layers for aviation safety, volcanic hazards and dispersion-model initialization. The v217 refresh confirms NCEI C01512, GOES-R product documentation and NOAA AWS open-data access paths, and clarifies that this is a 2 km ABI fixed-grid Full Disk product while GOES-16/17/18/19 cloud buckets are access routes rather than separate datasets.

GOES-R product page documents the VAA product family. AWS Registry lists NOAA GOES 16, 17, 18 and 19 buckets including noaa-goes19 with no-sign-request S3 access.

notes: Accepted as a distinct CLIMCAPS Level 3 gridded product. It is separate from v016 Level 2 rows because it provides 1 degree daily grids; specific quality control accepts valid profile levels down to the level where QC remains good.
desc_zh: SNDRAQIL3SDCCP V2 是 Aqua AIRS infrared-only 的 CLIMCAPS Level 3 daily 1 度格网产品，采用specific QC流程。它把 Level 2 红外/微波声探测反演汇总为可直接用于气候、环境和大气成分分析的格网变量，包括温度、水汽、臭氧、云、地表温度和红外发射率等。
desc_en: SNDRAQIL3SDCCP V2 is a CLIMCAPS Level 3 daily 1-degree gridded product for Aqua AIRS infrared-only, using the specific QC workflow. It aggregates Level 2 infrared/microwave sounder retrievals into analysis-ready gridded variables such as temperature, water vapor, ozone, clouds, surface temperature, and infrared emissivity.

notes: Accepted as a distinct ClimFiSP Level 3 gridded product derived via the ClimFiSP retrieval algorithm from CHIRP harmonized hyperspectral infrared radiances. It is separated from CLIMCAPS Level 3 QC products because the product family targets climate fingerprinting variables on a 0.5 degree grid.
desc_zh: ClimFiSP V2 提供 Level 3 日/月尺度格网大气、云和地表地球物理状态变量，包括温度廓线、水汽、臭氧、云属性、地表皮肤温度和红外发射率等。该产品由 CHIRP 气候超光谱红外辐射产品通过 ClimFiSP 反演算法生成，空间网格为 0.5 度，适合气候指纹和长期红外声探测分析。
desc_en: ClimFiSP V2 provides Level 3 daily or monthly gridded atmospheric, cloud, and surface geophysical state variables, including temperature profiles, water vapor, ozone, cloud properties, skin temperature, and infrared emissivity. The products are derived from CHIRP climate hyperspectral infrared radiances using the ClimFiSP retrieval algorithm on a 0.5-degree grid for climate fingerprinting and long-term infrared sounder analysis.

desc_zh: ClimFiSP V2 提供 Level 3 日/月尺度格网大气、云和地表地球物理状态变量，包括温度廓线、水汽、臭氧、云属性、地表皮肤温度和红外发射率等。该产品由 CHIRP 气候超光谱红外辐射产品通过 ClimFiSP 反演算法生成，空间网格为 0.5 度，适合气候指纹和长期红外声探测分析。
desc_en: ClimFiSP V2 provides Level 3 daily or monthly gridded atmospheric, cloud, and surface geophysical state variables, including temperature profiles, water vapor, ozone, cloud properties, skin temperature, and infrared emissivity. The products are derived from CHIRP climate hyperspectral infrared radiances using the ClimFiSP retrieval algorithm on a 0.5-degree grid for climate fingerprinting and long-term infrared sounder analysis.

coverage: Global primary CHIRP 13:30-orbit Level 1 radiance record using AIRS from 2002-09-01, S-NPP CrIS from 2016-09-01, and JPSS-1/NOAA-20 CrIS from 2018-09-01
license: NASA EOSDIS open data; cite provider DOI/catalog record
formats: NetCDF/HDF-style Level 1 radiance granules, Earthdata/OPeNDAP/cloud-enabled access where available

notes: Accepted as a CHIRP Level 1 climate hyperspectral infrared radiance product. CHIRP converts AIRS and CrIS parent radiances to a common spectral response function, removes inter-instrument radiometric offsets with SNPP CrIS as the reference, and preserves granule-level information needed for retrieval workflows. This is the primary continuous CHIRP record; supplemental AQCal, SNCal and J1Cal rows cover periods not included in this main record.
desc_zh: CHIRP V2 是 NASA Sounder SIPS 的 Level 1 气候高光谱红外辐射产品，把 EOS Aqua AIRS 与 Suomi-NPP/JPSS CrIS 的红外辐射调整到共同光谱响应函数，并去除跨仪器辐射偏差。它保留逐颗粒、逐视场的观测信息，是 ClimFiSP 和长期红外声探测气候记录的重要上游辐射数据。 该主产品提供从 2002 年起的连续 13:30 轨道辐射记录，按时间段衔接 AIRS、S-NPP CrIS 和 JPSS-1/NOAA-20 CrIS。
desc_en: CHIRP V2 is a NASA Sounder SIPS Level 1 climate hyperspectral infrared radiance product that maps EOS Aqua AIRS and Suomi-NPP/JPSS CrIS radiances to a common spectral response function and removes inter-instrument radiometric offsets. It preserves granule- and field-of-view-level information for retrievals and serves as an upstream radiance record for ClimFiSP and long-term infrared sounder climate analysis. This primary product provides the continuous 13:30-orbit record from 2002 onward, stitching AIRS, S-NPP CrIS, and JPSS-1/NOAA-20 CrIS across provider-defined time segments.

desc_zh: MTG FCI Cloud Type 是 Meteosat 第三代 FCI 的 Level 2 云类型产品，对已识别为有云的像元进行详细分类。它区分低云、中云、高云、不透明云、部分云和半透明云，并提供水相或冰相等云热力学相态信息，是云微物理、航空天气和辐射分析的重要补充。
desc_en: MTG FCI Cloud Type is a Meteosat Third Generation FCI Level 2 cloud-type product that gives a detailed classification for cloudy pixels. It distinguishes low-, mid-, and high-level clouds, opaque, fractional, and semi-transparent classes, and water or ice thermodynamic phase, complementing cloud microphysics, aviation weather, and radiation analysis.

notes: Accepted as an infrared-relevant MTG FCI Level 2 wind product. The catalogue API states that AMVs track clouds or water-vapor features between consecutive FCI images and use VIS 0.8, IR 3.8 night-only, IR 10.5, WV 6.3 and WV 7.3 channels; product cadence is every 30 minutes.
desc_zh: MTG FCI Atmospheric Motion Vectors 通过连续 FCI 图像中的云或水汽特征追踪生成风矢量，提供风速、风向、高度和质量信息。EUMETSAT 记录说明该产品使用 VIS 0.8、夜间 IR 3.8、IR 10.5、WV 6.3 和 WV 7.3 通道，每 30 分钟提供 BUFR/netCDF 数据。
desc_en: MTG FCI Atmospheric Motion Vectors derive wind vectors by tracking cloud or water-vapor features in consecutive FCI images, providing wind speed, direction, height, and quality information. The EUMETSAT record lists VIS 0.8, night-time IR 3.8, IR 10.5, WV 6.3, and WV 7.3 channels and BUFR/netCDF output every 30 minutes.

desc_en: MTG FCI Dust RGB is an official EUMETSAT infrared-channel RGB composite for monitoring dust-storm evolution and supporting cloud-type, thin-cirrus, low-level moisture, and volcanic-plume detection. It is based solely on infrared channels, so it is usable day and night; the catalogue record lists 2 km spatial resolution.

desc_en: MTG FCI Fire Temperature RGB helps identify the most intense fires and distinguish cooler from hotter fire signals. The EUMETSAT record explains that small or cooler fires mainly appear at 3.8 micrometres, while stronger fires gain 2.2 and 1.6 micrometre contributions; EUMETView generation is listed at 500 m resolution.

notes: Accepted as a distinct CM SAF Thematic Climate Data Record for surface radiation budget, latent heat flux and sensible heat flux. The EUMETSAT catalogue API states that radiation components are derived jointly from thermal and optical MVIRI/SEVIRI channels, with original thermal radiances inter-calibrated by EUMETSAT; turbulent fluxes use an adapted LSA SAF land surface model with the radiation components as input.
desc_zh: LANDFLUX Edition 1 是 CM SAF 基于 Meteosat 第一代 MVIRI 与第二代 SEVIRI 的地表辐射收支、潜热通量和感热通量气候数据记录，覆盖 1983-2020 年。其地表辐射收支分量由 MVIRI/SEVIRI 热红外与光学通道联合反演，热辐射经过 EUMETSAT 交叉定标，适合地表能量平衡、干旱、热浪和区域气候研究。
desc_en: LANDFLUX Edition 1 is a CM SAF climate data record of surface radiation budget, latent heat flux, and sensible heat flux from Meteosat First Generation MVIRI and Second Generation SEVIRI for 1983-2020. Its surface radiation budget components are jointly derived from thermal and optical channels, with original thermal radiances inter-calibrated by EUMETSAT, supporting surface-energy-balance, drought, heat-wave, and regional climate studies.

name: HIRS Level 1C Fundamental Data Record Release 2 Multimission Global

notes: Refreshed in v106 with canonical EUMETSAT Data Store product URL and API evidence. This is Release 2 of the Fundamental Data Record (FDR) brightness temperatures from the High Resolution Infrared Radiation Sounder (HIRS) on board NOAA and Metop satellites. The data record covers more than 40 years from 29 October 1978 to 31 December 2022. Release 2 provides recalibrated Level 1c brightness temperatures based on the V4.0 calibration method developed by Cao et al. (2007). This method was implemented into the NWP-SAF software ATOVS and AVHRR processing Package (AAPP). This software was consistently used to recalibrate and reprocess data from all HIRS instruments on board TIROS-N, NOAA-06 to NOAA-19, Metop-A, and Metop-B. Input HIRS data were collected from NOAA/CLASS and ECMWF archives and merged to produce a longer time series of some of the satellites.
desc_zh: HIRS Level 1C Fundamental Data Record Release 2 是 NOAA 与 Metop 多任务 HIRS 亮温基础数据记录，覆盖 1978 年 11 月至 2022 年 12 月。该产品基于 V4.0 标定方法重新处理 Level 1C 亮温，整合 TIROS-N、NOAA-06 至 NOAA-19、Metop-A 和 Metop-B 的 HIRS 仪器数据。
desc_en: HIRS Level 1C Fundamental Data Record Release 2 is a multimission NOAA and Metop HIRS brightness-temperature fundamental data record spanning November 1978 to December 2022. It provides recalibrated Level 1C brightness temperatures using the V4.0 calibration method and merges HIRS records from TIROS-N, NOAA-06 to NOAA-19, Metop-A and Metop-B.
openness: EUMETSAT free and unrestricted CC-BY-4.0 data policy
evidence: EUMETSAT browse API EO:EUM:DAT:0961 returned Dataset title 'HIRS Level 1C Fundamental Data Record Release 2 - Multimission - Global', date '1978-11-03/2022-12-31', rights ['NoConditions'], and abstract: This is Release 2 of the Fundamental Data Record (FDR) brightness temperatures from the High Resolution Infrared Radiation Sounder (HIRS) on board NOAA and Metop satellites. The data record covers more than 40 years from 29 October 1978 to 31 December 2022. Release 2 provides recalibrated Level 1c brightness temperatures based on the V4.0 calibration method developed by Cao et al. (2007). This method was implemented into the NWP-SAF software ATOVS and AVHRR processing Package (AAPP). This software was consistently used to recalibrate and reprocess data from all HIRS instruments on board TIROS-N, NOAA-06 to NOAA-19, Metop-A, and Metop-B. Input HIRS data were collected from NOAA/CLASS and ECMWF archives and merged to produce a longer time series of some of the satellites.

desc_zh: BIRDSAI Conservation Drones 是面向保护区巡护的长波热红外无人机视频数据集，包含南部非洲夜间动物和人员的真实与合成航拍 TIR 视频。数据提供 48 个真实视频、124 个合成视频、MOT 风格标注和多云端下载入口，适合热红外目标检测、跟踪和保护区遥感应用评测。
desc_en: BIRDSAI Conservation Drones is a long-wave thermal-infrared UAV video dataset for protected-area surveillance, containing real and synthetic nighttime aerial TIR videos of animals and humans in Southern Africa. It provides 48 real videos, 124 synthetic videos, MOT-style annotations and multiple cloud download routes, supporting thermal object detection, tracking and conservation remote-sensing benchmarks.
openness: CDLA permissive open data terms
evidence: LILA Conservation Drones page: BIRDSAI is long-wave TIR; contains nighttime animals and humans in Southern Africa; 48 real and 124 synthetic aerial TIR videos; 62k real and 100k synthetic images; MOT-style annotations; Google Cloud/AWS/Azure folders and ZIP downloads; CDLA permissive licence.

feature_count: 48 real aerial TIR videos, 124 synthetic aerial TIR videos; about 62k real and 100k synthetic images
file_size: ZIP downloads about 2.1 GB real train, 39.5 GB simulation train and 1.6 GB real test

It contains 959 frames, 20,647 masks and 239 tracks collected with a Parrot ANAFI Thermal UAV under different temperatures, weather conditions, viewing angles and flight heights.

desc_en: HIT-UAV is an open high-altitude UAV thermal-infrared object-detection dataset containing 2,898 infrared thermal images extracted from 43,470 video frames. It covers scenes such as schools, parking lots, roads and playgrounds, with person, bicycle, car and other-vehicle annotations, standard and oriented boxes, and metadata such as flight altitude, camera perspective and daylight intensity.

desc_zh: FY-4B AGRI L1 全圆盘数据是中国风云四号 B 星先进静止轨道辐射成像仪的一级产品，包含经过质量控制、导航和辐射定标处理的 HDF 数据。FY-4B AGRI 在 FY-4A 基础上提供 15 个通道，覆盖可见光、近红外、短波红外、中波红外和热红外，可用于云、大气廓线、长波辐射、火点、海温和灾害监测。
desc_en: FY-4B AGRI Level-1 full-disk data are HDF products from China’s Fengyun-4B Advanced Geostationary Radiation Imager after quality control, navigation, and radiometric calibration. FY-4B AGRI provides 15 channels spanning visible, near-infrared, shortwave infrared, midwave infrared, and thermal infrared observations for clouds, atmospheric sounding support, longwave radiation, fires, SST, and disaster monitoring.

NSMC API identifies FY-4B GIIRS as Geostationary Interferometric Infrared Sounder and says it detects atmospheric vertical structure by infrared hyperspectral interference spectroscopy. Product record lists China Regional L1 HDF at 12 km with 5,039,612 files and 42,568,304,717,184 bytes as of 2026-06-18; CMA CGMS paper states FY-4B GIIRS L1 data are open/free via multiple access routes, including Fengyun data center registration/download.
desc_zh: FY-4B GIIRS L1 是风云四号 B 星静止轨道干涉式红外探测仪的一级 HDF 数据，面向中国及周边区域的大气垂直探测。GIIRS 通过红外高光谱干涉分光获取温湿度廓线、臭氧廓线和总量等反演的上游观测，是全球少数地球静止红外高光谱探测数据源之一。
desc_en: FY-4B GIIRS Level-1 data are HDF observations from the Fengyun-4B Geostationary Interferometric Infrared Sounder over China and surrounding regions. GIIRS uses hyperspectral infrared interferometric measurements to support temperature, humidity, ozone profile, and total-column retrievals, making it one of the rare geostationary hyperspectral infrared sounding data sources.
openness: open/free access described by CMA/CGMS and provider terms
source_type: agency catalogue and national satellite data portal
evidence: EUMETSAT catalogue EO:EUM:DAT:0819; CMA CGMS-51 FY4 GIIRS L1 data paper; NSMC FY-4B GIIRS API snapshot

feature_count: 5,039,612 listed FY-4B GIIRS L1 files in NSMC API snapshot
file_size: 42,568,304,717,184 bytes listed in NSMC API snapshot

notes: Zenodo describes FlareSat as a benchmark Landsat 8 dataset for gas-flaring segmentation. Files include flare_patches.zip, flare_masks.zip, fire_patches.zip, fire_masks.zip, urban_patches.zip and urban_masks.zip, totaling about 10.5 GB on the landing page/API. This is accepted as a thermal-anomaly/gas-flaring benchmark, not as a canonical global flare-volume inventory.

desc_en: FlareSat is a Landsat 8 benchmark dataset for gas-flaring segmentation at oil and gas facilities. The Zenodo record documents 7,337 labeled 256 x 256 image patches covering 5,508 facilities across 94 countries, with flare, fire, and urban look-alike patch and mask archives for deep-learning training and evaluation.

desc_en: This Zenodo dataset supports a UAS optical and thermal infrared remote-sensing study of the La Fossa cone fumarole field on Vulcano Island. It includes thermal-anomaly polygon layers and related files used to analyze degassing, heat anomalies, and hydrothermal alteration structure, making it an open close-range volcanic thermal-infrared dataset.

It contains train, validation and evaluation splits saved as GeoTIFFs, with LST extracted from ESA LST CCI MODIS-AQUA and IRCDR products and auxiliary land-cover, DEM and vegetation-height guide rasters. Files include lst_train.zip, lst_val.zip, lst_eval.zip, MERGED_LC_aligned_lst.tif, MERGED_DEM_aligned_lst.tif and MERGED_vegetation_aligned_lst.tif.

desc_en: This Zenodo dataset supports guided land-surface-temperature super-resolution training, with the goal of downscaling AVHRR LST to MODIS LST spatial resolution. It provides train, validation, and evaluation GeoTIFF data plus aligned ESA LST CCI, land-cover, DEM, and vegetation-height auxiliary rasters for thermal-infrared LST super-resolution and downscaling experiments.

theme_canonical: European active fire thermal anomaly service
theme_raw: EFFIS active fires based on MODIS and VIIRS thermal anomalies with regional wildfire filtering
coverage: Europe, North Africa and Middle East EFFIS monitoring domain
license: European Commission reuse notice; no access limitations
formats: TIFF via EFFIS data download service according to JRC catalogue
access: JRC Data Catalogue landing page with EFFIS data download service
doi_or_citation: 10.2905/JRC.N660XDR; PID http://data.europa.eu/89h/e9d803fe-4426-4a45-9e6f-dd472de5c5f4
time_span: Near-real-time service updated about 6 times daily; v2-3-1 catalogue record current in 2026
notes: JRC describes this EFFIS dataset series as active fires for the European Forest Fire Information System. It uses MODIS and VIIRS thermal anomalies from NASA FIRMS and filters hotspots to support wildfire monitoring and subsequent burnt-perimeter mapping. The catalogue lists a Data Download Service, TIFF format, European Commission reuse conditions and no access limitations. Accepted as a regional Copernicus/JRC wildfire service distinct from the global GWIS row and broad NASA FIRMS row.
desc_zh: EFFIS Active Fires 是欧盟 JRC 欧洲森林火灾信息系统中的活动火点数据系列，基于 MODIS/VIIRS 热异常和 NASA FIRMS 火点，并面向欧洲及周边区域野火监测进行过滤和服务化发布。JRC 数据目录提供 TIFF 下载服务，无需注册访问，适合欧洲区域近实时火点和后续燃烧边界制图。
desc_en: EFFIS Active Fires is the European Commission JRC active-fire dataset series for the European Forest Fire Information System. It uses MODIS/VIIRS thermal anomalies from NASA FIRMS and regional filtering to support European wildfire monitoring and burnt-perimeter mapping, with TIFF download service and no access limitations.
openness: open
source_type: official data catalogue / active fire service
evidence: JRC Data Catalogue page e9d803fe-4426-4a45-9e6f-dd472de5c5f4: DOI 10.2905/JRC.N660XDR, active fires in EFFIS v2-3-1, MODIS/VIIRS thermal anomalies, 6 daily updates, data download service, TIFF format and no access limitations.
scale: EFFIS regional active fire service; MODIS 1 km and VIIRS 375 m source pixels; NRT updates
feature_count: dynamic EFFIS active-fire service layers
file_size: dynamic service; TIFF downloads vary by layer/query

name: NOAA Hazard Mapping System Fire and Smoke Product
provider: NOAA/NESDIS Satellite Analysis Branch / Office of Satellite and Product Operations
theme_canonical: North America active fire and smoke satellite analysis product
theme_raw: HMS active fire detections and smoke polygons from GOES, VIIRS, MODIS and AVHRR satellite data
coverage: North America / CONUS, Alaska and surrounding domains supported by NOAA HMS
license: NOAA public data / U.S. government public access; product page open
formats: Daily ASCII, shapefile ZIP and KML fire products; smoke shapefile/KML products according to HMS documentation
access: NOAA OSPO HMS product page and public FIRE web directory
doi_or_citation: NOAA OSPO Hazard Mapping System Fire and Smoke Product; public data directory https://satepsanone.nesdis.noaa.gov/pub/FIRE/web/HMS/
time_span: Forward-processing near-real-time service since 2002; fire and smoke data produced daily
notes: NOAA OSPO describes HMS as a near-real-time fire and smoke monitoring system using the best available satellite data at a given time. Product documentation lists GOES, VIIRS, MODIS and AVHRR active fire detection inputs, daily ASCII/shapefile/KML fire products, smoke shapefiles/KML and public data directory access. HMS outputs include attributes such as lon, lat, time, satellite, method, ecosystem and FRP. Accepted as a NOAA analyst/system product distinct from NASA FIRMS and single-sensor active fire products.
desc_zh: NOAA Hazard Mapping System Fire and Smoke Product 是 NOAA/NESDIS 面向北美的近实时火点与烟羽分析产品，综合 GOES、VIIRS、MODIS、AVHRR 等卫星火点输入和人工/系统分析。HMS 文档列出每日 ASCII、shapefile ZIP、KML 火点产品以及烟羽 shapefile/KML，属性包含经纬度、观测时间、卫星、检测方法、生态系统和 FRP。
desc_en: NOAA’s Hazard Mapping System Fire and Smoke Product is a near-real-time North American fire and smoke analysis system using GOES, VIIRS, MODIS, AVHRR and analyst/system processing. HMS documentation lists daily ASCII, shapefile ZIP and KML fire products, smoke polygons, public directory access and attributes such as location, time, satellite, detection method, ecosystem and FRP.
openness: open
source_type: official operational product / public data directory
evidence: NOAA OSPO HMS page: active fire detection data from GOES/VIIRS/MODIS/AVHRR, file naming and columns for ASCII/shapefile/KML fire products, smoke products, daily production schedule and public FIRE web directory.
scale: North America operational fire/smoke analysis; daily and continuously updated fire detections
feature_count: daily fire and smoke product files in public NOAA directory
file_size: dynamic daily files; size varies by date/product

name: CAMS Global Fire Assimilation System GFAS v1.2 Fire Emissions
provider: Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service / ECMWF
theme_canonical: Global fire radiative power and biomass burning emissions from MODIS thermal observations
theme_raw: CAMS global biomass burning emissions based on fire radiative power (GFAS)
coverage: Global, 0.1° x 0.1° regular latitude-longitude grid
license: CC-BY licence
formats: GRIB with optional conversion to NetCDF via Atmosphere Data Store API/download
access: ADS historical dataset page, ADS STAC metadata and ECMWF Data Portal transition notice; newer hourly GFAS 1.4.2 requires ECMWF Data Portal access
doi_or_citation: DOI: 10.24381/a05253c7; Kaiser et al. 2012, Biogeosciences, doi:10.5194/bg-9-527-2012
time_span: 2003-01-01 to 2025-12-04 for ADS-updated GFAS v1.2; later GFAS hourly 1.4.2 available via ECMWF Data Portal according to ECMWF announcement
notes: CAMS GFAS v1.2 converts MODIS Terra/Aqua fire radiative power observations into dry matter consumed and emissions for 40 pyrogenic species, and also provides wildfire radiative power and plume injection-height estimates. ADS metadata states global 0.1° gridded daily data, GRIB with optional NetCDF conversion, CC-BY licence and DOI. Accepted as a canonical official FRP-derived fire-emissions product distinct from active-fire point detections. Refreshed in v115: ADS now states that this GFAS v1.2 catalogue entry is no longer updated from 2025-12-04. ECMWF/CAMS forum guidance states that GFAS hourly 1.4.2 data are available from December 2025 through the ECMWF Data Portal (aux.ecmwf.int via SFTP/FTP/HTTPS), while ADS users should wait for a future GFAS 1.4.2 ADS catalogue entry. Keep this row as the DOI-backed GFAS v1.2 historical/open ADS record and a transition pointer rather than inventing an unverified new catalogue row.
desc_zh: CAMS GFAS v1.2 是 Copernicus/ECMWF 的全球火灾同化与排放产品，利用 Terra/Aqua MODIS 火辐射功率（FRP）热红外观测估算生物质燃烧位置、强度、干物质消耗和多种气体/气溶胶排放。v115 刷新确认：ADS 上的 GFAS v1.2 入口自 2025-12-04 起不再更新，后续 GFAS hourly 1.4.2 已转到 ECMWF Data Portal，并结合 MODIS 与 VIIRS 火观测。
desc_en: CAMS GFAS v1.2 is the Copernicus/ECMWF global fire assimilation and emissions product that converts Terra/Aqua MODIS fire radiative power observations into biomass-burning location, intensity, dry-matter consumption and emissions for many gases and aerosols. The v115 refresh confirms that the ADS GFAS v1.2 entry is no longer updated from 2025-12-04, while later GFAS hourly 1.4.2 access has moved to the ECMWF Data Portal and incorporates MODIS and VIIRS fire observations.
access_status: historical ADS record; latest GFAS access transitioned to ECMWF Data Portal
openness: open
evidence: ADS record lists GFAS satellite FRP basis, 40 pyrogenic species, 0.1° global daily grid, GRIB/NetCDF access, DOI 10.24381/a05253c7 and CC-BY licence. v115 refresh evidence: ADS overview says the dataset will no longer be updated from 2025-12-04; ECMWF forum announcement says GFAS daily 1.2 stops in CAMS_GFAS and GFAS hourly 1.4.2 is available from December 2025 from the ECMWF Data Portal; GWIS technical background says emissions switch from 2025-12-01 to GFAS v1.4.2 incorporating MODIS and VIIRS satellite observations.
scale: global 0.1 degree daily gridded
feature_count: 40 pyrogenic species plus FRP/plume variables
file_size: monthly/daily ADS files; size varies by request

name: NOAA Blended Global Biomass Burning Emissions Product Extended GBBEPx V3
provider: NOAA/NESDIS Office of Satellite Operations / NOAA NCEI
theme_canonical: Global blended biomass burning emissions from MODIS, VIIRS and geostationary fire observations
theme_raw: Blended Global Biomass Burning Emissions Product - Extended (GBBEPx) from Multiple Satellites
coverage: Global, 0.25° x 0.3125° and 0.1° x 0.1° grids; geostationary hourly fire-pixel products
license: NOAA public data; cite dataset; electronic downloads generally free
formats: netCDF-4 through NOAA CLASS/NCEI ordering; binary/FV3 and auxiliary outputs described by NCEI/OSPO
access: NOAA NCEI landing page with CLASS order/download route
doi_or_citation: NOAA NCEI dataset identifier gov.noaa.ncdc:C01560; GBBEPx V3
time_span: 2018-01-09 to present; ongoing continual updates according to NCEI metadata
notes: GBBEPx V3 blends MODIS QFED, VIIRS NPP/JPSS-1 fire emissions and geostationary GBBEP-Geo observations to produce daily global PM2.5, BC, CO, CO2, OC and SO2 emissions, plus hourly geostationary fire-pixel emissions and fire detection records. Accepted as an official NOAA operational emissions product derived from satellite active-fire observations.
desc_zh: NOAA GBBEPx V3 是 NOAA/NESDIS 和 NCEI 归档的全球生物质燃烧排放产品，融合 MODIS QFED、VIIRS NPP/JPSS-1 火点排放和地球同步卫星火观测。NCEI 元数据列出 0.25° x 0.3125° 与 0.1° x 0.1° 日尺度网格、netCDF-4 分发、持续更新以及 CLASS 订购/下载入口。
desc_en: NOAA GBBEPx V3 is an official blended global biomass-burning emissions product archived by NOAA/NESDIS and NCEI. It combines MODIS QFED, VIIRS NPP/JPSS-1 fire emissions and geostationary fire observations, with NCEI metadata describing daily global gridded emissions, netCDF-4 distribution, continual updates and CLASS access.
openness: open
evidence: NCEI landing page states daily global emissions, MODIS QFED + VIIRS + geostationary blending, netCDF-4 format, CLASS access, ongoing status and global bounds.
scale: global daily 0.1 degree and 0.25 x 0.3125 degree gridded
feature_count: PM2.5, BC, CO, CO2, OC, SO2 and quality/fire-detection auxiliary outputs
file_size: NOAA order-dependent; netCDF-4 files

name: NASA FEER v1.0-G1.2 Fire Energetics and Emissions Product
provider: NASA Goddard Space Flight Center Fire Energetics and Emissions Research
theme_canonical: Global fire emissions from GFAS fire radiative power and FEER emission coefficients
theme_raw: FEERv1.0 emissions inventory using GFASv1.2 FRP
coverage: Global, 0.1° x 0.1° daily grid for FEERv1.0-G1.2
license: Freely provided to the public; NASA FEER requests notification/citation when used
formats: Compressed NetCDF-4 annual/day/species files and getFEERdata shell script downloads
access: NASA GSFC FEER data page, FEER explorer/update page and direct annual/species NetCDF/script downloads where available
doi_or_citation: Ichoku and Ellison 2014, Atmos. Chem. Phys., doi:10.5194/acp-14-6643-2014; FEERv1.0-G1.2 data page
time_span: 2003 to 2025-12-04 for FEERv1.0-G1.2 stream; FEERv1.0-G1.4.2 begins production from 2025-12 according to FEER update page
notes: FEER provides a finished global emissions inventory generated from FEER emission coefficients and GFAS FRP. The FEER page documents FEERv1.0-G1.2 as daily 0.1° products from 2003 to present, compressed NetCDF-4 files, annual/species downloads and a getFEERdata script. Accepted as a NASA research data product that explicitly transforms satellite fire radiative energy/power into emissions. Refreshed in v115: the FEER update page reports that FEERv1.0-G1.2 emissions reached end of life on 2025-12-04 and FEERv1.0-G1.4.2 began production on 2026-03-19, beginning with December 2025. Keep this row as the existing FEER v1.0-G1.2 data-page record while documenting the transition to the GFAS/FEER 1.4.2 stream.
desc_zh: NASA FEER v1.0-G1.2 将 GFASv1.2 火辐射功率/火辐射能与 FEER 排放系数结合，形成全球日尺度火灾排放清单。v115 刷新确认：FEERv1.0-G1.2 于 2025-12-04 结束生产，FEERv1.0-G1.4.2 从 2025 年 12 月开始生产，后续早期日期将逐步补齐。
desc_en: NASA FEER v1.0-G1.2 combines GFASv1.2 fire radiative power/energy with FEER emission coefficients to produce a global daily fire-emissions inventory. The v115 refresh confirms that FEERv1.0-G1.2 reached end of life on 2025-12-04 and FEERv1.0-G1.4.2 began production from December 2025, with earlier dates to be added later.
access_status: historical FEERv1.0-G1.2 stream; newer FEERv1.0-G1.4.2 production started
openness: open
evidence: FEER data page documents FRP/FRE-based emissions, GFASv1.2 input, daily 0.1° global FEERv1.0-G1.2 products, compressed NetCDF-4 and direct/yearly/script downloads. v115 refresh evidence: NASA GSFC FEER update page states FEERv1.0-G1.2 emissions reached end of life on 04.Dec.2025 and FEERv1.0-G1.4.2 begins production beginning with December 2025.
scale: global 0.1 degree daily gridded
feature_count: multiple aerosol and trace-gas species listed by FEER species file
file_size: annual/species NetCDF files; size varies by year/species

name: NASA GMAO Quick Fire Emissions Dataset QFED
provider: NASA Global Modeling and Assimilation Office / GEOS-ESM
theme_canonical: Global FRP-derived fire emissions and gridded fire radiative power
theme_raw: Quick Fire Emissions Dataset (QFED) Level 3A/3B
coverage: Global; daily near-real-time and historical gridded products from March 2000 to present
license: NASA public research dataset; GEOS-ESM QFED code/documentation under Apache-2.0; cite QFED documentation
formats: Gridded Level 3 products; historical data via NASA NCCS datashare; code/documentation on GitHub
access: NASA/GEOS-ESM QFED repository plus NASA NCCS public datashare for historical products
doi_or_citation: Darmenov and da Silva, The Quick Fire Emissions Dataset (QFED): Documentation of versions 2.1, 2.2 and 2.4; NASA GMAO
time_span: March 2000 to present for extended historical dataset; near-real-time daily emissions also maintained
notes: QFED uses satellite-retrieved fire radiative power to estimate global fire emissions. The GEOS-ESM README identifies Level 3A gridded FRP stratified by biome and Level 3B gridded biomass-burning emissions, with both near-real-time operational and extended historical systems. Literature/documentation and data-share references describe MODIS fire products as the core input and daily products at 0.3125° x 0.25° and 0.1° resolutions in recent versions.
desc_zh: NASA GMAO QFED 使用卫星反演的火辐射功率（FRP）估算全球火灾排放，Level 3A 包含按生物群系分层的网格 FRP，Level 3B 包含多种生物质燃烧排放。官方 GEOS-ESM 仓库说明同时维护近实时日排放系统和自 2000 年 3 月起的历史产品，相关文档指向 MODIS 火产品输入和 NASA NCCS 数据共享。
desc_en: NASA GMAO QFED estimates global fire emissions from satellite-retrieved fire radiative power. The official GEOS-ESM repository describes Level 3A gridded FRP by biome, Level 3B gridded biomass-burning emissions, near-real-time daily production and an extended historical dataset from March 2000 onward, with documentation pointing to MODIS fire inputs and NASA NCCS data sharing.
openness: open
source_type: NASA modeling data product / code repository
evidence: GEOS-ESM QFED README states FRP-derived emissions, Level 3A/3B products, near-real-time and historical systems; QFED documentation describes MODIS FRP inputs and public historical data availability.
scale: global daily gridded, commonly 0.3125 x 0.25 degree and 0.1 degree in recent versions
feature_count: FRP plus multiple pyrogenic emissions species such as BC, OC, CO, CO2, PM2.5, NH3, NOx, CH4
file_size: historical datashare files; size varies by period/version

name: NCAR Fire INventory from NCAR FINN v2.5
provider: NSF NCAR Atmospheric Chemistry Observations and Modeling / GDEX / Zenodo
theme_canonical: Global daily fire emissions from MODIS and VIIRS active-fire thermal anomalies
theme_raw: FINNv2.5 Fire Inventory from NCAR
coverage: Global; 1 km fire-spot text files and 0.1° x 0.1° gridded annual files
license: Publicly available research dataset; Zenodo records are open and citable
formats: Yearly text files, compressed text, NetCDF/gridded files depending on speciation and GDEX/Zenodo package
access: NSF NCAR GDEX dataset landing page, NCAR FINN page and Zenodo downloadable records
doi_or_citation: Wiedinmyer et al. 2023, Geosci. Model Dev., doi:10.5194/gmd-16-3873-2023; GDEX d312009; Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.7868652 family
time_span: 2002-2019 MODIS-only and 2012-2019 MODIS+VIIRS in FINNv2.5 published products; NCAR page also documents full-period MODIS and MODIS+VIIRS variants
notes: FINNv2.5 estimates global daily fire emissions using satellite detections of active fires/thermal anomalies and land cover. NCAR/GDEX documentation states MODIS fire detections for the long time series and MODIS+VIIRS active fire detections from 2012 onward, with 0.1° gridded annual files and 1 km fire-spot text files. Accepted as an open research fire-emissions inventory with explicit thermal-anomaly inputs.
desc_zh: FINN v2.5 是 NCAR 的全球每日火灾排放清单，利用 MODIS 以及 2012 年后的 MODIS+VIIRS 主动火点热异常检测、土地覆盖和排放因子估算多种气体与气溶胶排放。GDEX/Zenodo 页面提供 0.1° 网格年文件和 1 km 火点文本文件，适合大气化学、空气质量和火灾排放研究。
desc_en: FINN v2.5 is NCAR’s global daily fire-emissions inventory built from MODIS and, from 2012 onward, MODIS+VIIRS active-fire thermal-anomaly detections, land cover and emission factors. GDEX/Zenodo records provide 0.1° gridded annual files and 1 km fire-spot text files for atmospheric chemistry, air-quality and fire-emissions studies.
openness: open
evidence: NCAR/GDEX states FINN uses satellite active-fire thermal anomalies, MODIS and MODIS+VIIRS variants, 0.1° gridded files and 1 km fire-spot text outputs; Zenodo records provide downloadable packages.
scale: global daily, 1 km fire spots and 0.1 degree gridded products
feature_count: trace gases, aerosols and VOC speciation mechanisms
file_size: tens of GB for full Zenodo/GDEX packages; individual yearly files vary

name: Global Ensemble Fire Emission Product EnsemFire V1.0
provider: Li et al. / NOAA-funded partners / Springer Nature Figshare
theme_canonical: Global ensemble fire emissions and FRP from multiple fire-emissions inventories
theme_raw: EnsemFire V1.0 daily global biomass burning emission dataset
coverage: Global, 0.1° x 0.1° regular latitude-longitude grid
license: CC BY according to Springer Nature Figshare dataset page; article in NOAA repository under CC BY-NC-ND for manuscript
formats: Daily NetCDF files, one file per date, downloadable as 25.84 GB Figshare package
access: Springer Nature Figshare dataset DOI and download-all package
doi_or_citation: Dataset DOI: 10.6084/m9.figshare.29642135; article DOI: 10.1038/s41597-025-06429-z
notes: EnsemFire V1.0 is a 2026 Figshare/Springer Nature dataset linked to a 2025 Scientific Data article. The dataset integrates GFAS, FINN, FEER, QFED, GBBEPx, EPA and CFFEPS to provide ensemble mean and standard deviation fields for emissions and FRP on a global 0.1° grid. Accepted as an open derivative ensemble dataset because its member inventories include multiple FRP/thermal-anomaly satellite products and it directly includes FRP.
desc_zh: EnsemFire V1.0 是全球 0.1° 日尺度生物质燃烧排放集合数据集，整合 GFAS、FINN、FEER、QFED、GBBEPx、EPA 和 CFFEPS 等清单。Figshare 页面提供 2012-2021 年 NetCDF 文件，总包约 25.84 GB，变量包括 PM2.5、BC、OC、CO、NH3、NOx、SO2、CO2、CH4 和 FRP 及其集合不确定性。
desc_en: EnsemFire V1.0 is a global 0.1° daily ensemble biomass-burning emissions dataset integrating GFAS, FINN, FEER, QFED, GBBEPx, EPA and CFFEPS. The Figshare record provides 2012-2021 NetCDF files in a 25.84 GB package, including PM2.5, BC, OC, CO, NH3, NOx, SO2, CO2, CH4, FRP and ensemble-spread fields.
openness: open
evidence: Springer Nature Figshare record states daily global 0.1° NetCDF, 2012-2021, 25.84 GB download, CC BY, FRP and emissions variables, and integration of seven inventories including GFAS/FINN/FEER/QFED/GBBEPx.
scale: global 0.1 degree daily gridded
feature_count: PM2.5, BC, OC, CO, NH3, NOx, SO2, CO2, CH4, FRP and standard deviations
file_size: 25.84 GB download-all package

name: NASA ORNL DAAC Global Fire Emissions Database GFEDv4
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / Global Fire Data project
theme_canonical: Global burned area and fire emissions using MODIS active-fire and burned-area observations
theme_raw: Global Fire Emissions Database Version 4 (GFEDv4/GFED4.1s)
coverage: Global, 0.25° x 0.25° grid with regional summary files
license: NASA ORNL DAAC open Earthdata distribution; cite DOI and dataset; Earthdata access terms apply
formats: HDF, HDF5, text and CSV emission-factor files
access: NASA ORNL DAAC dataset landing page and DOI data access
doi_or_citation: Randerson et al. 2018, Global Fire Emissions Database Version 4, ORNL DAAC, doi:10.3334/ORNLDAAC/1293
time_span: Monthly data 1995-06 to 2015-02; annual/emissions 1997-2014; daily and 3-hourly scalars for selected periods according to ORNL DAAC guide
notes: GFEDv4/GFED4.1s is primarily a fire-emissions and burned-area product rather than a pure thermal-infrared product. It is included because the ORNL DAAC guide documents use of satellite fire activity, VIRS/ATSR/MODIS active-fire detections and one-kilometre MODIS thermal anomalies to estimate small-fire influence, alongside MODIS burned-area products and CASA-GFED modeling. Marked as boundary/derived infrared relevance rather than a core FRP product.
desc_zh: GFEDv4/GFED4.1s 是 NASA ORNL DAAC 归档的全球火灾排放与燃烧面积数据集，提供 HDF/HDF5/text/CSV 文件和 DOI。它不是纯热红外产品，但方法说明包含 VIRS、ATSR、MODIS 主动火点/热异常与 MODIS 燃烧面积数据，尤其用于估计小火贡献，因此作为“热异常参与的火排放派生数据”收录。
desc_en: GFEDv4/GFED4.1s is a NASA ORNL DAAC global burned-area and fire-emissions dataset with DOI-backed HDF/HDF5/text/CSV files. It is not a pure thermal-infrared product, but the documentation uses VIRS, ATSR and MODIS active-fire/thermal-anomaly information together with MODIS burned-area products and CASA-GFED modeling, so it is cataloged as a boundary derived fire-emissions dataset with thermal-anomaly inputs.
openness: open
source_type: NASA DAAC archive / derived fire-emissions dataset
evidence: ORNL DAAC guide lists data products, DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/1293, HDF/HDF5/text/CSV files, global 0.25° grid and methodology using MODIS active-fire/thermal anomalies for small-fire estimates.
scale: global 0.25 degree monthly with daily/3-hourly scalars
feature_count: burned area, emissions, biosphere fluxes, emission factors and regional summaries
file_size: 1,029 files according to ORNL DAAC guide

name: NASA ORNL DAAC Annual Wildland Fire Emissions WFEIS v0.5 CONUS and Alaska
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / Michigan Tech Research Institute WFEIS / NASA Carbon Monitoring System
theme_canonical: Annual wildland fire emissions modeled from MODIS-supported fire data for CONUS and Alaska
theme_raw: Annual wildland fire emissions (WFEIS v0.5) for Conterminous US and Alaska, 2001-2013
coverage: Conterminous United States and Alaska; 0.01° (~1 km) grid
license: NASA Earthdata open data; U.S. government works / cite DOI and dataset
formats: GeoTIFF via ORNL DAAC/Earthdata, ZIP bundle, ISO metadata and PDF documentation
access: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC landing page, DOI, CMR and Earthdata Search access
doi_or_citation: doi:10.3334/ORNLDAAC/1306; ORNL DAAC CMS_WFEIS_CONUS-AK_1306
notes: This ORNL DAAC product archives annual modeled wildland fire emissions from WFEIS v0.5 at approximately 1 km resolution for CONUS and Alaska. Earthdata metadata lists Terra/MODIS, GeoTIFF format, 117 granules, 279.13 MB size, emissions species and biomass/fuel/consumption layers. Accepted as a regional modeled fire-emissions dataset with MODIS-linked fire input lineage, distinct from the live WFEIS calculator.
desc_zh: 该 ORNL DAAC 数据集归档了 WFEIS v0.5 对美国本土和阿拉斯加 2001-2013 年野火排放的年度 0.01°（约 1 km）估计。Earthdata 记录列出 Terra/MODIS、GeoTIFF、117 个文件/颗粒、DOI 和公开访问，并包含 C、CO、CO2、CH4、NMHC、PM 以及生物量、燃料可用量和消耗估计。
desc_en: This ORNL DAAC dataset archives annual WFEIS v0.5 modeled wildland-fire emissions for CONUS and Alaska from 2001-2013 at 0.01° (~1 km) resolution. The Earthdata record lists Terra/MODIS, GeoTIFF format, 117 granules, a DOI and open access, with C, CO, CO2, CH4, NMHC, PM, biomass, fuel availability and consumption estimates.
openness: open
source_type: NASA DAAC archive / modeled fire-emissions dataset
evidence: Earthdata record lists DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/1306, Terra/MODIS, GeoTIFF, 279.13 MB, 117 granules, public/open access and WFEIS emissions variables.
scale: regional 0.01 degree annual gridded
feature_count: 117 granules; emissions and fuel/consumption variables

name: ABoVE Wildfire Date of Burning within Fire Scars across Alaska and Canada
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / ABoVE / Loboda, Hall and Baer
theme_canonical: Daily wildfire progression polygons from MODIS active-fire detections
theme_raw: ABoVE: Wildfire Date of Burning within Fire Scars across Alaska and Canada, 2001-2019
coverage: Alaska and Canada; fire scar polygons and associated MODIS active-fire points
license: NASA Earthdata open data; cite DOI and dataset
formats: Shapefile via ORNL DAAC/Earthdata with user guide and CMR metadata
access: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC landing page, DOI and Earthdata Search access
doi_or_citation: Loboda, Hall and Baer 2017/2021, ORNL DAAC, doi:10.3334/ORNLDAAC/1559
notes: The dataset maps date of burning within fire scars across Alaska and Canada. Earthdata describes the DoB algorithm as identifying first fire occurrence from MODIS MCD14ML Collection 6 active-fire detections and deriving daily fire progression polygons; associated MODIS active-fire points are also provided. Accepted as a direct MODIS thermal-anomaly-derived fire-dynamics product.
desc_zh: ABoVE Wildfire Date of Burning 数据集提供 Alaska 和 Canada 火疤内部的每日燃烧日期/火场推进多边形。Earthdata 说明其 DoB 算法利用 MODIS MCD14ML Collection 6 主动火点热异常识别首次起火并推导火场每日推进，同时提供关联的 MODIS 主动火点。
desc_en: The ABoVE Wildfire Date of Burning dataset provides daily date-of-burning and fire-progression polygons within fire scars across Alaska and Canada. Earthdata states that the DoB algorithm uses MODIS MCD14ML Collection 6 active-fire detections to identify first fire occurrence and derive daily progression, with associated MODIS active-fire points included.
openness: open
source_type: NASA DAAC archive / MODIS active-fire derived product
evidence: Earthdata record lists shapefile format, 727.324 MB, 38 granules, DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/1559, MODIS instrument and associated MODIS active-fire points.
scale: regional polygon product, daily progression within 2001-2019 fire scars
feature_count: 38 granules

name: ABoVE Burned Area Depth and Combustion for Alaska and Canada
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / ABoVE / Potter et al.
theme_canonical: Boreal burned area, burn depth and carbon combustion using MODIS active-fire and Landsat observations
theme_raw: ABoVE: Burned Area, Depth, and Combustion for Alaska and Canada, 2001-2019
coverage: Alaska and Canada; approximately 500 m / 463 m ABoVE grid
license: NASA Earthdata open data; cite DOI and dataset
formats: Cloud-optimized GeoTIFF via ORNL DAAC/Earthdata
access: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC landing page, DOI and Earthdata Search access
doi_or_citation: Potter et al. 2022, ORNL DAAC, doi:10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2063; related Biogeosciences paper on burned area and carbon emissions
notes: This ABoVE-FED product provides annual gridded fire locations, burn fraction, carbon combustion and burn depth for Alaska and Canada. Earthdata states fire locations and date of burn were detected by MODIS-derived active-fire products, burned area was primarily estimated with Landsat dNBR and upscaled to MODIS resolution, and COG files are available. Accepted as a boreal fire-carbon product with explicit MODIS thermal active-fire inputs and Landsat/TIRS project lineage.
desc_zh: ABoVE Burned Area, Depth, and Combustion 数据集提供 Alaska 和 Canada 2001-2019 年约 500 m 年度火点/燃烧比例、碳燃烧量和燃烧深度估计。Earthdata 说明火点位置和燃烧日期由 MODIS 主动火产品检测，燃烧面积主要来自 Landsat dNBR，并提供 COG 格式与 DOI。
desc_en: The ABoVE Burned Area, Depth, and Combustion dataset provides annual ~500 m fire-location, burn-fraction, carbon-combustion and burn-depth estimates for Alaska and Canada from 2001-2019. Earthdata states that fire locations and date of burn were detected from MODIS-derived active-fire products, burned area was primarily estimated from Landsat dNBR, and COG files are available with a DOI.
openness: open
source_type: NASA DAAC archive / boreal fire carbon product
evidence: Earthdata record lists DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2063, COG format, 1.279 GB, 38 granules, MODIS/Landsat instruments and MODIS-derived active-fire detection lineage.
scale: regional ~500 m annual gridded
feature_count: 38 granules

name: Arctic Boreal Annual Burned Area ABBA V2 Circumpolar Boreal Forest and Tundra
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / ABoVE / Loboda et al.
theme_canonical: Circumpolar MODIS-based burned area for boreal forest and tundra
theme_raw: Arctic Boreal Annual Burned Area, Circumpolar Boreal Forest and Tundra, V2, 2002-2022
coverage: Circumpolar land regions above 50°N; 463 m cloud-optimized GeoTIFF
license: NASA Earthdata open data; cite DOI and dataset
formats: Cloud-optimized GeoTIFF via ORNL DAAC/Earthdata
access: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC landing page, DOI and Earthdata Search access
doi_or_citation: Loboda et al. 2024, ORNL DAAC, doi:10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2328
notes: ABBA V2 maps annual cumulative end-of-season burned area for circumpolar boreal forests and tundra. Earthdata/ORNL documentation states the product was generated with the Arctic Boreal Burned Area algorithm using MODIS Collection 6 products and dNBR, with 21 COG files at 463 m. Accepted as a MODIS-derived boreal burned-area product; relevance is burned-area/thermal-fire monitoring rather than raw thermal radiance.
desc_zh: ABBA V2 提供 2002-2022 年环北极 50°N 以北针叶林和苔原年度累计燃烧面积。ORNL/NASA 说明该产品使用 MODIS Collection 6 数据和 dNBR 的 Arctic Boreal Burned Area 算法生成，提供 463 m COG 文件和 DOI。
desc_en: ABBA V2 provides annual cumulative end-of-season burned area for circumpolar boreal forests and tundra north of 50°N from 2002-2022. ORNL/NASA documentation states that it uses MODIS Collection 6 products and the dNBR-based Arctic Boreal Burned Area algorithm, with 463 m COG files and a DOI.
openness: open
source_type: NASA DAAC archive / MODIS-derived burned-area product
evidence: Earthdata and ORNL guide list DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2328, MODIS Collection 6, dNBR algorithm, 21 COG files, 463 m resolution and 2002-2022 coverage.
scale: circumpolar 463 m annual gridded
feature_count: 21 annual COG files
file_size: about 9.36 MB from ORNL fire dataset listing

name: NASA ORNL DAAC Fire Intensity and Burn Severity Metrics for Circumpolar Boreal Forests
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / Rogers, Soja, Goulden and Randerson
theme_canonical: Circumpolar boreal fire intensity, FRP, burn severity and post-fire surface metrics
theme_raw: Fire Intensity and Burn Severity Metrics for Circumpolar Boreal Forests, 2001-2013
coverage: Circumpolar boreal forests, 40°N to 80°N; quarter, half and one degree grids
license: NASA Earthdata open data; cite DOI and dataset
formats: GeoTIFF via ORNL DAAC/Earthdata with user guide
access: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC landing page, DOI and Earthdata Search access
doi_or_citation: Rogers et al. 2017, ORNL DAAC, doi:10.3334/ORNLDAAC/1520
notes: This ORNL DAAC dataset includes fire intensity represented by fire radiative power (FRP), land surface temperature, albedo, tree-cover change, NBR, NDVI and derived burn-severity metrics for circumpolar boreal forests. Earthdata lists MODIS on Terra/Aqua, GeoTIFF format, 42 granules and DOI. Accepted as a direct FRP/LST-containing boreal fire-severity product.
desc_zh: 该 ORNL DAAC 数据集面向北美和欧亚环北极针叶林，提供 2001-2013 年火强度、燃烧严重度和火后地表变化指标。数据明确包含火辐射功率（FRP）和地表温度（LST），同时包括 albedo、树盖变化、NBR、NDVI、冠层焦枯、植被破坏和火致树木死亡等指标。
desc_en: This ORNL DAAC dataset provides fire intensity, burn severity and post-fire surface-change metrics for circumpolar boreal forests from 2001-2013. It explicitly includes fire radiative power (FRP) and land surface temperature (LST), along with albedo, tree-cover change, NBR, NDVI, crown scorch, vegetation destruction and fire-induced tree mortality metrics.
openness: open
source_type: NASA DAAC archive / FRP and burn-severity product
evidence: Earthdata record lists DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/1520, GeoTIFF, 3.375 MB, 42 granules, MODIS instruments and variables including FRP and land surface temperature.
scale: circumpolar boreal quarter/half/one degree gridded
feature_count: 42 granules

name: ECOSTRESS Swath Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity Instantaneous L2 Global 70 m V002
provider: NASA LP DAAC; NASA/JPL ECOSTRESS
theme_canonical: Satellite thermal infrared land surface temperature and emissivity
theme_raw: ECOSTRESS ECO_L2_LSTE V002 swath LST&E from five TIR bands
coverage: Global land between approximately 54°S and 54°N; ISS ECOSTRESS observations
license: NASA EOSDIS data openly shared without restriction; cite dataset and observe Earthdata access terms
formats: HDF5
access: NASA Earthdata / LP DAAC cloud-enabled dataset; Earthdata Search, API and LP DAAC tools
doi_or_citation: 10.5067/ECOSTRESS/ECO_L2_LSTE.002; Hook, S., & Hulley, G. (2022). ECOSTRESS Swath Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity Instantaneous L2 Global 70 m v002 [Dataset]. NASA LP DAAC.
notes: NASA Earthdata lists ECO_L2_LSTE v002 as cloud enabled, CMR C2076114664-LPCLOUD, HDF5, 70 m, 126.3 MB per listed dataset-size field, 802010 files/granules, active. It provides atmospherically corrected LST and emissivity derived from five TIR bands using a physics-based TES algorithm; layers include LST, emissivity bands 1-5, LST/error, emissivity error, wideband emissivity, PWV, QC, cloud mask and water mask. Kept separate from older ECO2LSTE V001 because v002 has a distinct DOI, product code, cloud-enabled collection, added cloud/water mask layers, and coexists with v002 gridded/tiled successors.
desc_zh: ECOSTRESS ECO_L2_LSTE v002 是 NASA LP DAAC 发布的 ISS 搭载热红外 L2 地表温度与发射率条带产品，空间分辨率约 70 m，覆盖约 54°S-54°N 的全球陆地。产品基于五个 TIR 波段和 TES 算法反演 LST&E，并包含质量控制、误差、宽波段发射率、云/水掩膜等层，适合植物水分胁迫、城市热环境、火险前兆和热异常研究。
desc_en: ECOSTRESS ECO_L2_LSTE v002 is the NASA LP DAAC swath Level-2 land surface temperature and emissivity product from the ISS-mounted thermal infrared ECOSTRESS instrument. It provides roughly 70 m atmospherically corrected LST&E from five TIR bands with TES retrievals plus QC, uncertainty, wideband emissivity, cloud and water masks for global land observations between about 54°S and 54°N.
openness: Open NASA EOSDIS data; free access with Earthdata Login for download/API workflows
evidence: NASA Earthdata catalog confirms DOI 10.5067/ECOSTRESS/ECO_L2_LSTE.002, LP DAAC center, cloud-enabled status, HDF5 format, 70 m resolution, 2018-07-09 to Present temporal extent, and open EOSDIS citation guidance.
scale: Global land 70 m swath thermal infrared L2
feature_count: 802010 files/granules listed by NASA Earthdata on 2026-06-18
file_size: 126.3 MB dataset-size field on Earthdata catalog page

name: ECOSTRESS Gridded Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity Instantaneous L2 Global 70 m V002
provider: NASA LP DAAC; NASA/JPL ECOSTRESS
theme_canonical: Satellite thermal infrared land surface temperature and emissivity
theme_raw: ECOSTRESS ECO_L2G_LSTE V002 globally snapped gridded LST&E
coverage: Global land between approximately 54°S and 54°N; globally snapped 0.0006° grid
license: NASA EOSDIS data openly shared without restriction; cite dataset and observe Earthdata access terms
formats: HDF5
access: NASA Earthdata / LP DAAC cloud-enabled dataset; Earthdata Search, API and LP DAAC tools
doi_or_citation: 10.5067/ECOSTRESS/ECO_L2G_LSTE.002; Hook, S., & Hulley, G. (2023). ECOSTRESS Gridded Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity Instantaneous L2 Global 70 m v002 [Dataset]. NASA LP DAAC.
notes: NASA Earthdata lists ECO_L2G_LSTE v002 as cloud enabled, CMR C2076113037-LPCLOUD, HDF5, 70 m, 270.8 MB per listed dataset-size field, 480627 files/granules, active. It is a gridded version of ECO_L2_LSTE v002 resampled with nearest neighbor to a globally snapped 0.0006 degree grid, with layers for LST, LST error, wideband emissivity, height, view zenith, QC, cloud mask and water mask. Accepted as a distinct canonical product because it has its own DOI/product code and provides an analysis-ready grid packaging rather than raw swath geometry.
desc_zh: ECOSTRESS ECO_L2G_LSTE v002 是 L2 LST&E 的全球网格版本，把条带观测重采样到 0.0006° 的全球对齐网格，空间分辨率约 70 m。它保留地表温度、误差、宽波段发射率、高程、观测天顶角、质量标志以及云/水掩膜，适合需要统一网格坐标的时序分析和区域拼接。
desc_en: ECOSTRESS ECO_L2G_LSTE v002 repackages the ECOSTRESS Level-2 LST&E retrieval onto a globally snapped 0.0006° grid at about 70 m resolution. The HDF5 product carries LST, LST error, wideband emissivity, height, view zenith, QC, cloud and water masks, making it a practical grid-based route for time-series and regional analysis.
openness: Open NASA EOSDIS data; free access with Earthdata Login for download/API workflows
evidence: NASA Earthdata catalog confirms DOI 10.5067/ECOSTRESS/ECO_L2G_LSTE.002, LP DAAC center, cloud-enabled status, HDF5 format, 70 m resolution, 2018-07-09 to Present extent, gridded relationship to ECO_L2_LSTE, and open EOSDIS citation guidance.
scale: Global land 70 m gridded thermal infrared L2
feature_count: 480627 files/granules listed by NASA Earthdata on 2026-06-18
file_size: 270.8 MB dataset-size field on Earthdata catalog page

name: ECOSTRESS Tiled Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity Instantaneous L2 Global 70 m V002
provider: NASA LP DAAC; NASA/JPL ECOSTRESS
theme_canonical: Satellite thermal infrared land surface temperature and emissivity
theme_raw: ECOSTRESS ECO_L2T_LSTE V002 MGRS-tiled COG LST&E
coverage: Global land between approximately 54°S and 54°N; modified MGRS/UTM tiles about 109.8 km by 109.8 km
license: NASA EOSDIS data openly shared without restriction; cite dataset and observe Earthdata access terms
formats: Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF (COG)
access: NASA Earthdata / LP DAAC cloud-enabled dataset; Earthdata Search, API, cloud COG access and LP DAAC tools
doi_or_citation: 10.5067/ECOSTRESS/ECO_L2T_LSTE.002; Hook, S., & Hulley, G. (2022). ECOSTRESS Tiled Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity Instantaneous L2 Global 70 m v002 [Dataset]. NASA LP DAAC.
notes: NASA Earthdata lists ECO_L2T_LSTE v002 as cloud enabled, CMR C2076090826-LPCLOUD, COG, 70 m, 7.3 MB per listed dataset-size field, 15108888 files/granules, active. The product subsets ECO_L2G_LSTE into modified MGRS/UTM tiles of about 109.8 km by 109.8 km, with each band as a separate COG; layers include LST, LST error, wideband emissivity, QC, height, cloud mask and water mask. Accepted as a distinct product because COG tile packaging is a direct open cloud workflow for GIS/STAC-like access and differs operationally from HDF5 swath/grid collections.
desc_zh: ECOSTRESS ECO_L2T_LSTE v002 是面向云端和 GIS 使用的 MGRS/UTM 分幅 COG 版 L2 地表温度与发射率产品。它从 ECO_L2G_LSTE 网格产品裁切成约 109.8 km 方形瓦片，每个波段以独立 Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF 分发，便于按区域直接下载、读取和可视化。
desc_en: ECOSTRESS ECO_L2T_LSTE v002 is the cloud-optimized tiled version of the ECOSTRESS Level-2 LST&E product. It subsets the gridded product into modified MGRS/UTM tiles of about 109.8 km by 109.8 km and distributes each layer as a separate COG, enabling direct regional access in GIS and cloud workflows.
openness: Open NASA EOSDIS data; free access with Earthdata Login for download/API workflows
evidence: NASA Earthdata catalog confirms DOI 10.5067/ECOSTRESS/ECO_L2T_LSTE.002, LP DAAC center, cloud-enabled status, COG format, 70 m resolution, modified MGRS tiling, 2018-07-09 to Present temporal extent, and open EOSDIS citation guidance.
scale: Global land 70 m tiled COG thermal infrared L2
feature_count: 15108888 files/granules listed by NASA Earthdata on 2026-06-18
file_size: 7.3 MB dataset-size field on Earthdata catalog page

name: SHIFT HyTES L1 Brightness Temperature March 23 2022
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / SHIFT / HyTES team
theme_canonical: Airborne hyperspectral thermal infrared brightness temperature
theme_raw: HyTES Level 1B brightness temperature from SHIFT Santa Barbara airborne campaign
coverage: Santa Barbara County, California, USA and nearby ocean; SHIFT flight scenes on 2022-03-23; spatial extent N 34.8859 S 34.18 E -118.784 W -120.603
license: NASA EOSDIS data openly shared without restriction; cite dataset and observe Earthdata access terms
formats: HDF5; ENVI georeferencing files; KML boundaries/overlays; quicklook images; spectral response data
access: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC cloud-enabled dataset; Earthdata Search/API and ORNL DAAC access routes
doi_or_citation: 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2245; Rivera, G., Johnson, W. R., Freepartner, R. J., Eng, B. T., & Hook, S. J. (2023). SHIFT: HyTES L1 Brightness Temperature, March 23 2022 (Version 1). ORNL Distributed Active Archive Center.
notes: NASA Earthdata lists this ORNL DAAC dataset as cloud enabled, CMR C2813448589-ORNL_CLOUD, complete, processing level 1B, 132.032 GB, 548 files/granules, published and updated 2023-12-06. HyTES acquired 256 spectral bands between 7.5 and 12 micrometers from a NOAA Twin Otter during the SHIFT campaign; the dataset contains 91 flight scenes with HDF5 brightness temperature, pixel georeferencing in HDF5 and ENVI, KML boundaries/overlay imagery, quicklooks and spectral response data. Accepted as distinct from the existing SHIFT HyTES L2 row because this is the upstream L1B brightness-temperature archive rather than derived surface emissivity and LST.
desc_zh: SHIFT HyTES L1 亮温数据集收录了 2022 年 3 月 23 日 HyTES 在 Santa Barbara 地区飞行获取的航空高光谱热红外观测。HyTES 覆盖 7.5-12 微米的 256 个热红外波段，数据以 HDF5 亮温形式提供，并配套 ENVI 像元地理定位、KML 边界/叠加图、quicklook 和光谱响应信息，可作为 LST、发射率、潜热通量和污染源识别等反演的基础输入。
desc_en: The SHIFT HyTES L1 Brightness Temperature dataset contains airborne hyperspectral thermal infrared observations collected over the Santa Barbara, California campaign area on March 23, 2022. HyTES measures 256 bands from 7.5 to 12 micrometers; the archive provides HDF5 brightness temperature for 91 flight scenes plus pixel georeferencing, ENVI/KML support files, quicklooks and spectral response data for downstream LST, emissivity and flux retrievals.
openness: Open NASA EOSDIS data; free access with Earthdata Login for download/API workflows
evidence: NASA Earthdata catalog confirms DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2245, ORNL DAAC center, cloud-enabled status, complete data state, 132.032 GB size, 548 files/granules, HyTES/Twin Otter platform, 2022-03-23 extent, HDF5 data and open EOSDIS citation guidance.
scale: Regional airborne hyperspectral TIR campaign; 91 flight scenes; 7.5-12 micrometers
feature_count: 548 files/granules listed by NASA Earthdata on 2026-06-18; 91 flight scenes described in catalog
file_size: 132.032 GB dataset-size field on Earthdata catalog page

name: MASTER Geological Earth Mapping Experiment GEMx California-Arizona Summer 2023
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / MASTER project
theme_canonical: Airborne multispectral visible-to-thermal infrared radiance and derived LST/emissivity
theme_raw: MASTER GEMx Summer 2023 L1B radiance and L2 thermal emissivity/LST campaign data
coverage: California, Oregon, Nevada and Arizona, USA; spatial extent N 45.09 S 31.865 E -112.49 W -129.38; 13 NASA ER-2 flights
license: NASA EOSDIS data openly shared without restriction; cite dataset and observe Earthdata access terms
formats: HDF-4 L1B; ENVI and KMZ L2 where provided; flight paths; browse images; ancillary documentation
access: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC cloud-enabled dataset; Earthdata Search/API and ORNL DAAC documentation/download routes
doi_or_citation: 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2319; Hook, S. J., Myers, J. S., Thome, K. J., Fitzgerald, M., Kahle, A. B., Airborne Sensor Facility NASA Ames Research Center, & Kokaly, R. F. (2024). MASTER: Geological Earth Mapping Experiment (GEMx), California-Arizona, Summer 2023 (Version 1). ORNL Distributed Active Archive Center.
notes: NASA Earthdata lists this ORNL DAAC MASTER campaign collection as cloud enabled, CMR C2905518711-ORNL_CLOUD, complete, processing level 2, 83.776 GB, 993 files/granules. MASTER provides 50 bands from visible to thermal infrared wavelengths (0.460-12.879 micrometers) at approximately 50 m spatial resolution. Where L2 products are described, derived emissivity covers five thermal infrared bands from 8.58 to 12.13 micrometers and land surface temperature; L1B is HDF-4 and L2 products are ENVI/KMZ. Accepted as a campaign-level row with its own DOI/CMR/time-span, complementary to but not duplicating the global MASTER Level 2 Emissivity and LST row.
desc_zh: MASTER GEMx 2023 夏季数据集收录了 NASA ER-2 在美国西南干旱与半干旱区 13 次飞行获取的航空多光谱观测。数据包含 0.460-12.879 微米 50 个波段的 L1B 定标辐亮度，以及 8.58-12.13 微米热红外发射率和地表温度等 L2 产品，可用于关键矿产制图、地表热属性和生态/地质解译。
desc_en: The MASTER GEMx Summer 2023 dataset contains airborne observations from 13 NASA ER-2 flights over the southwestern United States. It includes 50-band L1B calibrated radiance from 0.460 to 12.879 micrometers and derived thermal infrared emissivity and land surface temperature products for critical-minerals mapping and surface thermal studies.

evidence: NASA Earthdata catalog confirms DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2319, ORNL DAAC center, cloud-enabled status, 83.776 GB size, 993 files/granules, temporal extent 2023-04-25 to 2023-09-26, MASTER instrument, NASA ER-2 platform, and open EOSDIS citation guidance.

desc_en: WiSARD is a University of Washington UAV visual-thermal dataset for wilderness search and rescue across diverse Washington State environments. desc_en: UMA-SAR is a University of Málaga multimodal dataset collected from an all-terrain vehicle during realistic outdoor SAR training exercises in 2018 and 2019. It includes overlapping RGB and thermal infrared cameras, LiDAR, IMU and GPS streams for RGB-TIR fusion, SAR object/person detection, scene understanding and localization/mapping in unstructured environments.
openness: Open dataset landing page with public download route; reuse governed by stated project/repository terms
evidence: UMA page states the sensor suite included overlapping RGB and thermal infrared cameras, Velodyne HDL-32 LiDAR, IMU and GPS; four SAR exercise sequences total 5.2 km and 77 min, plus three empty-site sequences; data are offered in human-readable format and ROS bag files with sequence ZIP/ROSBAG links; page states research-use with citation.

notes: Accepted as the current NOAA/NCEI IR Sounder UTH BT CDR. It extends the HIRS channel-12 upper-tropospheric humidity brightness-temperature record using HIRS and IASI-equivalent HIRS channel-12 observations; older HIRS Ch12 v3.2 metadata was reviewed but deferred as legacy/overlapping.
desc_zh: 该 NOAA/NCEI 气候数据记录提供红外探测器上对流层湿度亮温，基于 HIRS 星间校准的 12 通道亮温，并结合 IASI 等效 HIRS 12 通道观测扩展时间序列。产品为全球月尺度 2.5° 网格 NetCDF-4 数据，覆盖 1978 年至今，适合上对流层水汽、长期气候变率和模式评估研究。
desc_en: This NOAA/NCEI Climate Data Record provides infrared-sounder upper-tropospheric humidity brightness temperature based on intersatellite-calibrated HIRS channel-12 observations and IASI-derived equivalent HIRS channel-12 measurements. The NetCDF-4 monthly global 2.5 degree record spans 1978 to present and supports upper-tropospheric water-vapor variability, climate monitoring and model evaluation.

source_type: official NOAA NCEI Climate Data Record
evidence: NCEI product page lists configuration item 01B-03, HIRS level-1b input, cloud-clearing, limb-correction and inter-satellite calibration, global 2.5 x 2.5 degree coverage from 1978-present, DOI 10.25921/mwe8-7e12, direct download and THREDDS. Metadata C01691 lists Version 4.0, NetCDF-4, total volume about 25 MB, monthly update frequency, and source HIRS/IASI records.

Its infrared content includes AVHRR 3.75, 11 and 12 micron brightness temperatures plus HIRS 6.7 micron and CO2-channel brightness temperatures on a global 0.1 degree grid from 1981 to present.

provider: NOAA NCEI / NOAA CLASS / NOAA POES / EUMETSAT MetOp
theme_canonical: HIRS infrared sounder Level 1B radiance archive
theme_raw: High Resolution Infrared Radiation Sounder (HIRS/4) Level 1B Data from NOAA and EUMETSAT
coverage: Global polar-orbiting swath archive from NOAA-15/16/17/18/19 and MetOp-A/B/C, 1998-05-13 to present, about 10 km resolution
license: NOAA open data; cite dataset; free electronic download in most cases; CLASS order workflow may require account
formats: BUFR Level 1B geolocated radiances and reflectivity via NOAA CLASS order
access: NCEI metadata landing page with NOAA CLASS data search/order link
doi_or_citation: gov.noaa.ncdc:C00321; gov.noaa.class:TOVS; cite as HIRS 1B Data from NOAA and EUMETSAT
notes: Accepted as the official NOAA/EUMETSAT HIRS/4 Level 1B source archive. It is distinct from HIRS-derived CDRs such as UTH BT and OLR because it contains geolocated Level 1B radiances/reflectivity used to generate higher-level atmospheric products.
desc_zh: 该 NOAA/NCEI 元数据记录指向 NOAA CLASS 分发的 HIRS/4 Level 1B 数据，覆盖 NOAA POES 与 EUMETSAT MetOp 极轨卫星上的高分辨率红外探测器观测。HIRS 有 20 个通道，主要用于大气温度和水汽探测，Level 1B 产品提供约 10 km 分辨率的定位辐射和反射率，是 UTH、OLR 等红外气候记录的上游源档案。
desc_en: This NOAA/NCEI metadata record points to HIRS/4 Level 1B data distributed through NOAA CLASS for NOAA POES and EUMETSAT MetOp polar-orbiting satellites. HIRS has 20 channels for atmospheric temperature and water-vapor sounding, and the Level 1B archive provides geolocated radiances and reflectivity at about 10 km resolution as an upstream source for UTH, OLR and other infrared climate products.

NCEI C00683 lists identifiers gov.noaa.class:AVHRR/gov.noaa.ncdc:C00683, NOAA Level 1B format and time period 1978-11-05 to present.

coverage: Full-disk gridded coverage, 85E-205E (155W), 60N-60S
license: CEReS/JMA academic non-commercial data policy; redistribution to third parties prohibited except among research communities
formats: Anonymous FTP bzip2 binary grids; EXT/VIS/SIR/TIR 2-byte counts plus 4 km physical-variable products including TIR brightness temperature
access: CEReS release note and anonymous FTP server; quasi-real-time data may require IP-based access approval
doi_or_citation: Takenaka et al. (2020) doi:10.3390/rs12152472; Yamamoto et al. (2020) doi:10.3390/rs12091372; acknowledge CEReS Himawari 8/9 gridded data
time_span: Himawari-8 AHI from 2015-07-07 to 2022-12-13; Himawari-9 AHI from 2022-12-13 onward, per CEReS release note
notes: CEReS Version 02 is a gridded latitude-longitude full-disk release distinct from the existing JAXA P-Tree and AWS raw/open-distribution rows. The release note documents TIR bands 01-10 mapped to JMA AHI bands 13,14,15,16,07,08,09,10,11,12 at 0.02 degree, plus new 4 km physical-variable files including TIR brightness temperature.
desc_zh: 千叶大学 CEReS 发布的 Himawari-8/9 AHI 全圆盘格网数据 Version 02，把 JMA Himawari 标准数据重采样到经纬度格网，并提供短波红外和热红外通道。热红外部分覆盖 AHI 3.9 至 13.3 微米相关通道，含 0.02 度格网以及 4 km 物理量产品，适合区域云、辐射、地表和灾害监测研究。
desc_en: The CEReS Himawari-8/9 AHI gridded full-disk Version 02 dataset remaps JMA Himawari Standard Data onto latitude-longitude grids and includes shortwave and thermal infrared channels. Its thermal infrared component spans AHI channels from about 3.9 to 13.3 micrometers, with 0.02-degree grids and 4 km physical-variable products suitable for cloud, radiation, surface and hazard studies.
openness: academic_noncommercial_with_restrictions
evidence: CEReS Version 02 release note documents anonymous FTP access, full-disk domain, TIR band mapping, 4 km physical-variable products, citation instructions and data policy.
scale: full-disk 10-minute geostationary gridded archive
feature_count: EXT/VIS/SIR/TIR band families plus 4KM physical-variable and geometry products; collection grows by timestamp/month
file_size: product-dependent; bzip2-compressed binary grids

desc_zh: NASA ASDC 的 CERES GEO Edition 4 Himawari-8 北半球产品，使用 LaRC SatCORPS 算法从 Himawari-8 AHI 观测反演云微物理和辐射属性。数据为小时级 netCDF-4 文件，覆盖 2015-07-05 至 2022-12-01，可通过 Earthdata Search、直接下载和 OPeNDAP 获取。
desc_en: NASA ASDC CERES GEO Edition 4 Himawari-8 Northern Hemisphere v1.2 provides hourly cloud microphysical and radiation-property retrievals derived from Himawari-8 AHI using LaRC SatCORPS algorithms. The netCDF-4 files cover 2015-07-05 to 2022-12-01 and are accessible through Earthdata Search, direct download and OPeNDAP.

evidence: NASA ASDC landing page lists DOI, entry ID, Himawari-8/AHI platform, netCDF-4 format, hourly retrieval description, spatial coverage and temporal coverage.

feature_count: hourly files with cloud/radiation variables; ASDC granule count product-dependent
file_size: product-dependent

name: NJIAS Himawari-8/9 Cloud Feature Dataset HCFD 0.04Deg
provider: Nanjing Joint Institute for Atmospheric Sciences / Science Data Bank / Himawari-8/9 AHI
theme_canonical: Cloud properties / Himawari infrared and multispectral retrievals
theme_raw: NJIAS HCFD-0.04Deg; Science Data Bank dataset; ESSD 2024 data description
coverage: East Asia and west North Pacific; 0.04 degree regular latitude-longitude grids
license: Science Data Bank dataset terms; ESSD article is CC BY 4.0; dataset landing page should be checked for exact reuse terms
formats: Science Data Bank downloadable dataset files; gridded 0.04 degree cloud-feature products
access: Science Data Bank dataset landing page with DOI and downloadable files; ESSD article provides methodology and data-availability references
doi_or_citation: 10.57760/sciencedb.17574; related ESSD paper doi:10.5194/essd-16-1747-2024
time_span: 2016-2025 per current Science Data Bank landing page snippet; ESSD 2024 paper describes the 2016-04 to 2022-12 seven-year version
notes: Accepted as a peer-reviewed, repository-backed Himawari cloud-feature dataset. ESSD describes 30 variables at 0.5 h interval, including cloud mask, cloud-top phase, cloud-top height, optical thickness, effective radius, snow, dust and haze masks.
desc_zh: NJIAS HCFD 0.04Deg 是基于 Himawari-8/9 AHI 多光谱和红外观测构建的云特征数据集，覆盖东亚和西北太平洋。ESSD 论文描述其包含云掩膜、云顶相态、云顶高度、光学厚度、粒子有效半径以及雪、沙尘、霾等掩膜变量，时间分辨率为 30 分钟，是红外云参数研究的重要开放资料。
desc_en: NJIAS HCFD 0.04Deg is a Himawari-8/9 AHI cloud-feature dataset for East Asia and the western North Pacific, built from multispectral and infrared observations. The ESSD paper describes 30 variables at 30-minute intervals, including cloud mask, cloud-top phase, cloud-top height, optical thickness, effective radius and snow, dust and haze masks.
openness: open_repository_terms
evidence: Science Data Bank landing page for HCFD-0.04Deg; ESSD 2024 paper doi:10.5194/essd-16-1747-2024 describes variables, spatial/temporal coverage and repository DOIs.
scale: regional 30-minute gridded multi-year cloud-feature archive
feature_count: 30 cloud-feature variables at 0.04 degree and 30-minute interval
file_size: repository file sizes vary by part/year

provider: Nanjing Joint Institute for Atmospheric Sciences / Science Data Bank / Himawari-8/9 AHI

license: Science Data Bank dataset terms; check landing page for exact reuse terms

doi_or_citation: 10.57760/sciencedb.09945; Science Data Bank HTCFD Part I landing page
notes: Science Data Bank describes HTCFD as radiance and cloud features within 20 degree by 20 degree boxes around WNP typhoon centers. Later literature describes 30-minute temporal and 0.02 degree spatial resolution with visible/infrared observations and cloud variables.
desc_zh: NJIAS HTCFD Part I 面向 2016-2021 年西北太平洋台风，围绕台风中心裁剪 20 度乘 20 度区域，提供 Himawari-8/9 AHI 辐射和云特征变量。它将高频红外观测与台风场景绑定，适合研究台风云系结构、强度变化和降水相关特征。
desc_en: NJIAS HTCFD Part I covers western North Pacific typhoons from 2016 to 2021 using 20-degree by 20-degree boxes centered on storm positions. It packages Himawari-8/9 AHI radiance and cloud-feature variables, connecting high-frequency infrared observations with typhoon structure, intensity and precipitation studies.
openness: open_repository_terms
evidence: Science Data Bank HTCFD Part I landing page; ESSD and follow-on literature cite HTCFD as Himawari radiance/cloud-feature typhoon dataset.
scale: typhoon-centered multi-year event archive
feature_count: radiance and cloud-feature variables for WNP typhoon-centered boxes
file_size: repository file sizes vary by storm/year

name: KMA NMSC GK2A AMI Official L1B and L2 Data Service
provider: Korea Meteorological Administration / National Meteorological Satellite Center / Geo-KOMPSAT-2A AMI
theme_canonical: Geostationary infrared imagery and derived meteorological products
theme_raw: GK2A AMI L1B channels and L2 products via NMSC OpenAPI, FTP, DCPC and web-based services
coverage: Asia-Pacific full disk centered near 128.2E; Korean Peninsula/local rapid-scan areas; product-dependent domains
license: KMA/NMSC data-service terms; account/API key or agency registration may be required depending on access route
formats: NetCDF for GK2A AMI L1B and L2 products; service-dependent download/API packaging
access: NMSC official web pages, OpenAPI service, real-time FTP for eligible NMHS users, DCPC and web-based download; AWS/NOAA mirror exists as a separate open cloud route
doi_or_citation: KMA/NMSC GK2A official service pages; CGMS-50 KMA-WP-03 report on GK2A data services
notes: Accepted as the official NMSC/KMA service boundary distinct from the existing AWS noaa-gk2a-pds mirror. NMSC states GK2A generates 52 meteorological products and provides information through web pages, Open API, FTP and DCPC. The CGMS-50 KMA report states GK2A produces 16-channel L1B and meteorological data in NetCDF, with full disk every 10 minutes and local/rapid observations every 2 minutes. NMSC OpenAPI product-list pages were browser-readable during v089 but returned HTTP 503 to lychee, so the stable NMSC GK2A page and CGMS report are used as verified evidence URLs. Refreshed in v118: official/search evidence confirms KMA/NMSC forest-fire/wild-fire product-family activity, but no stable product-specific public landing/download URL for an individual GK2A L2 fire product was verified. AWS open bucket inspection also showed no L2 product prefix. The row remains the official service/product-family boundary rather than a product-specific fire dataset row.
desc_zh: KMA/NMSC 的 GK2A AMI 官方数据服务是韩国 Geo-KOMPSAT-2A 气象成像仪的权威分发入口，覆盖 16 个 L1B 通道和多类 L2 气象产品。红外相关内容包括 3.8、6.3、6.9、7.3、8.7、9.6、10.5、11.2、12.3、13.3 微米通道，以及云顶性质、云检测、红外大气运动矢量、雾、火点、地表温度、海表温度、长波辐射等派生产品。
desc_en: The KMA/NMSC GK2A AMI official data service is the authoritative distribution boundary for Korea Geo-KOMPSAT-2A meteorological imager data, covering 16 L1B channels and many L2 meteorological products. Infrared-relevant content includes the 3.8, 6.3, 6.9, 7.3, 8.7, 9.6, 10.5, 11.2, 12.3 and 13.3 micrometer channels, plus derived products such as cloud-top properties, cloud detection, infrared atmospheric motion vectors, fog, fire, land surface temperature, sea surface temperature and longwave radiation.
openness: official_service_with_registration_options
evidence: NMSC GK2A official page states 52 meteorological products and service through web pages, Open API, FTP and DCPC; NMSC user-readiness page lists AMI infrared bands; CGMS-50 KMA-WP-03 documents NetCDF L1B/L2 distribution, OpenAPI availability and product list. v118 evidence: web/source probes found KMA/NMSC materials mentioning GK2A forest fire/wild fire products and open AWS GK2A data; direct S3 list-type=2 probes found only L1B imagery prefixes, not L2 fire-product prefixes; prior v089 evidence remains the verified official service boundary.
scale: operational geostationary full-disk and rapid-scan service
feature_count: 16 L1B AMI channels plus 66 AMI OpenAPI service items cited by KMA report, including many infrared-derived L2 products
file_size: full-disk L1B visible channels up to hundreds of MB; infrared channels about 58 MB per full-disk channel in KMA report; L2 product-dependent
