license: CMA/NSMC Fengyun Satellite Data Service terms; public users can access most Fengyun satellite data free of charge under CMA conditions, registration/tooling may be required
formats: CSV, HDF via Fengyun Satellite Data Service; NSMC API product records include HDF full-disk normalized imagery and DAT stretched/compressed imagery where available
access: Fengyun Satellite Data Service search/order/download portal and API metadata; registration and download toolkit may be required for bulk access
doi_or_citation: NSMC DataPortal FY2H VISSR L1 product records; Fengyun Satellite Data Service
time_span: 2018-06-05 to 2026-06-20; FY-2H satellite period 2018-06-05 onward in NSMC metadata
notes: Accepted as a FY-2 VISSR geostationary visible/infrared L1 archive not covered by existing FY-4A/FY-4B rows. NSMC API lists 2 L1 VISSR product records for FY2H: FY-2H VISSR L1 data (Normalized/Full Disk); FY-2H VISSR L1 data (Compressed/Full Disk). Summed API snapshot counts: 265,561 files and 22,611,803,730,302 bytes as of 2026-06-20. The instrument is Visible and Infrared Spin Scan-Radiometer; product comments indicate 5000M, while resolution fields are 1KM in API records, so users should follow NSMC product documentation for exact channel geometry.
desc_zh: FY2H VISSR Level-1 数据是风云二号静止气象卫星的可见光/红外自旋扫描辐射仪档案，补足了 FY-4A/B 之前和并行时期的中国静止轨道红外观测主干。NSMC 数据服务列出全圆盘标称图像、展宽图像或压缩展宽图像等 L1 产品，可用于亚洲-太平洋区域云、对流、天气过程和长期静止轨道红外观测研究。
desc_en: FY2H VISSR Level-1 data form part of the Fengyun-2 geostationary visible/infrared spin-scan radiometer archive, extending the Chinese geostationary infrared backbone beyond the existing FY-4A/B rows. NSMC lists normalized full-disk, stretched and/or compressed L1 products that support Asia-Pacific cloud, convection, weather-system and long-term geostationary infrared studies.
openness: public_portal_with_conditions
evidence: NSMC satellite and product API endpoints for FY2H; verified DataPortal structure page; Fengyun Satellite Data Service home page; CGMS Fengyun data service update noting public users can access most Fengyun data free of charge under CMA conditions.
scale: multi-year geostationary full-disk L1 archive
feature_count: 265,561 listed L1 VISSR files across 2 product records in NSMC API snapshot
file_size: 22,611,803,730,302 bytes summed in NSMC API snapshot; product/file dependent

formats: HDF-5 Level-2B SST products; MOSDAC DOI pages describe VHRR IR-derived SST and daily averaged SST products
access: NASA CMR metadata with MOSDAC Atom dataset link; MOSDAC DOI/product pages for SST and daily SST; download through MOSDAC services may require account/session
doi_or_citation: CMR concept C1214622582-ISRO; MOSDAC datasetId K1-VHR-SST; MOSDAC DOI pages /doi/5/ and /doi/6/
notes: Accepted as an official Kalpana-1 thermal-infrared derived ocean product. CMR describes the collection as Kalpana-1 VHRR Level-2B Sea Surface Temperature in HDF-5 format. MOSDAC DOI pages describe both daily averaged SST over the previous 24 hours and Level-2 retrieved SST derived using the VHRR IR band.
desc_zh: Kalpana-1 VHRR Level-2B 海表温度产品是基于 Kalpana VHRR 红外通道反演的印度洋区域 SST 数据。CMR 记录给出 HDF-5 格式和 2008-06-01 起始时间，MOSDAC DOI 页面进一步说明该产品包含红外通道反演 SST 和 24 小时日平均 SST，补足了 INSAT-3D 之前印度静止轨道热红外海洋产品链。
desc_en: The Kalpana-1 VHRR Level-2B Sea Surface Temperature collection provides Indian Ocean SST retrievals derived from the Kalpana VHRR infrared channel. CMR lists HDF-5 format and a 2008-06-01 start date, while MOSDAC DOI pages describe both IR-derived Level-2 SST and 24-hour daily averaged SST products, filling a pre-INSAT-3D geostationary thermal-infrared ocean record.
openness: public_metadata_no_cost_data
evidence: CMR C1214622582-ISRO metadata; MOSDAC Atom link datasetId=K1-VHR-SST; MOSDAC DOI pages for KALPANA1-VHRR-SST and KALPANA1-VHRR-DAILYSST.
scale: historical geostationary Level-2B SST archive
feature_count: CMR collection; granule count available through CMR OpenSearch, not enumerated in v091
file_size: granule-dependent

name: INSAT-3DS Radiance Albedo Brightness Temperature Level 1C
provider: ISRO / MOSDAC / EUMETSAT Third-Party Data Service
theme_canonical: Geostationary infrared imagery / Level 1C radiance and brightness temperature
theme_raw: INSAT3D-L1C; Radiance/Albedo/Brightness Temperature Level 1C - INSAT-3DS; EO:EUM:DAT:INSAT:INSAT3D-L1C
coverage: INSAT-3DS geostationary sector over India, Indian Ocean and surrounding regions; longitude about 82E per WMO OSCAR
license: ISRO/MOSDAC and EUMETSAT third-party data terms; registration/licence acceptance may be required
formats: INSAT-3DS Level 1C image products with rectified counts, radiance and brightness temperature; albedo for visible channel; format via EUMETSAT/MOSDAC product metadata
access: EUMETSAT User Portal/Data Store third-party catalogue; MOSDAC mission/data-access routes as official ISRO context
doi_or_citation: EO:EUM:DAT:INSAT:INSAT3D-L1C; EUMETSAT INSAT3D-L1C collection reference
time_span: INSAT-3DS distribution from 2025-03-26 according to EUMETSAT search snippet; satellite launched 2024-02-17 per WMO OSCAR
notes: Accepted as a distinct INSAT-3DS third-party EUMETSAT collection, not a duplicate of existing INSAT-3D rows. EUMETSAT search evidence states that from 26/03/2025 INSAT-3DS data are distributed and that Level 1C products store rectified image data as counts, radiance and brightness temperatures, with albedo for the visible channel. MOSDAC states INSAT-3DS has the same sensors/products as INSAT-3D and produces L1B calibrated/geolocated data before product generation.
desc_zh: INSAT-3DS Level 1C 辐射/反照率/亮温集合是印度新一代 INSAT-3D 第二重复星的成像基础产品，通过 EUMETSAT 第三方数据服务提供稳定目录入口。该产品把校正后的影像数据表示为计数、辐射和亮温，红外亮温通道可用于印度及印度洋区域云、对流、天气系统和地表/海洋参数反演。
desc_en: The INSAT-3DS Level 1C Radiance/Albedo/Brightness Temperature collection is a core imager product from India’s INSAT-3D second-repeat mission, exposed through EUMETSAT’s third-party data service. The rectified image data include counts, radiance and brightness temperatures, making the infrared channels useful for clouds, convection, weather systems and surface/ocean retrievals over India and the Indian Ocean region.
openness: third_party_catalogue_with_registration
evidence: EUMETSAT user portal collection EO:EUM:DAT:INSAT:INSAT3D-L1C; search snippets describing L1C counts/radiance/brightness temperature; MOSDAC INSAT-3DS mission page; WMO OSCAR INSAT-3DS page.
scale: operational geostationary Level 1C image collection
feature_count: EUMETSAT collection; live product count not exposed through browse API during v092
file_size: product-dependent

name: INSAT-3DS Sea Surface Temperature Product
provider: ISRO / MOSDAC / EUMETSAT Third-Party Data Service
theme_canonical: Geostationary sea surface temperature / thermal infrared retrieval
theme_raw: INSAT3D-SST; INSAT-3DS SST; EO:EUM:DAT:1018
coverage: INSAT-3DS geostationary sector over the Indian Ocean and surrounding regions; product-dependent ocean coverage
license: ISRO/MOSDAC and EUMETSAT third-party data terms; registration/licence acceptance may be required
formats: INSAT-3DS SST products via EUMETSAT User Portal/Data Store; exact file format via product metadata
access: EUMETSAT User Portal/Data Store third-party catalogue; MOSDAC mission/data-access routes as official ISRO context
doi_or_citation: EO:EUM:DAT:1018; EUMETSAT INSAT3D-SST collection reference
time_span: INSAT-3DS product era; satellite launched 2024-02-17 per WMO OSCAR and EUMETSAT collection active as third-party INSAT-3DS SST
notes: Accepted as a distinct INSAT-3DS thermal-infrared derived SST collection. EUMETSAT identifies this product as ISRO INSAT-3DS Sea Surface Temperature, acronym INSAT3D-SST, collection reference EO:EUM:DAT:1018. It complements earlier INSAT-3D and Kalpana SST rows rather than replacing them.
desc_zh: INSAT-3DS 海表温度产品是基于印度 INSAT-3DS 静止轨道成像观测生成的热红外海洋参数集合，通过 EUMETSAT 第三方数据目录提供可访问入口。它延续 Kalpana-1 和 INSAT-3D 的印度洋静止轨道 SST 产品链，可用于海气交换、热带天气和海洋监测。
desc_en: The INSAT-3DS Sea Surface Temperature product is a thermal-infrared ocean retrieval collection from India’s INSAT-3DS geostationary imager, exposed through EUMETSAT’s third-party catalogue. It extends the Indian Ocean geostationary SST product line represented by Kalpana-1 and INSAT-3D, supporting air-sea interaction, tropical weather and ocean monitoring studies.
openness: third_party_catalogue_with_registration
evidence: EUMETSAT user portal collection EO:EUM:DAT:1018; search snippets identifying ISRO INSAT-3DS SST, acronym INSAT3D-SST and collection reference; MOSDAC INSAT-3DS mission page; WMO OSCAR INSAT-3DS page.
scale: operational geostationary thermal-infrared SST collection
feature_count: EUMETSAT collection; live product count not exposed through browse API during v092
file_size: product-dependent

name: Storm Event Imagery SEVIR Deep Learning Dataset
provider: MIT AI Accelerator / AWS Open Data / NOAA GOES-16 ABI, NEXRAD and GLM
theme_canonical: Benchmark / severe storm imagery and infrared nowcasting
theme_raw: Storm EVent ImageRy (SEVIR); GOES-16 ABI + NEXRAD + GLM
coverage: Continental United States storm events centered on GOES-16 observations
license: AWS Open Data Registry states no restrictions on use of the data
formats: HDF5 event files and catalogue metadata in public S3; Python utilities/examples available
access: AWS Open Data public S3 bucket s3://sevir with no AWS account required; documentation and code on GitHub
doi_or_citation: Veillette et al. (2020), SEVIR: A Storm Event Imagery Dataset for Deep Learning Applications in Radar and Satellite Meteorology, NeurIPS Datasets and Benchmarks; cite AWS registry access URL
time_span: GOES-16 storm-event archive; events added yearly according to AWS registry
notes: Accepted as a canonical GOES-16 severe-weather benchmark dataset. AWS describes SEVIR as spatially and temporally aligned GOES-16 ABI satellite imagery, NEXRAD radar mosaics and GOES-16 GLM lightning detections. It is directly relevant to infrared because SEVIR includes GOES-16 ABI channels used in deep-learning nowcasting and storm-event imagery tasks.
desc_zh: SEVIR 是面向深度学习气象应用的风暴事件影像数据集，将 GOES-16 ABI 卫星影像、NEXRAD 雷达拼图和 GOES-16 GLM 闪电观测进行时空配准。它通过 AWS Open Data 的公开 S3 桶分发，可用于降水临近预报、合成雷达、闪电/强对流识别等任务，是地球静止红外 AI benchmark 的核心数据集之一。
desc_en: SEVIR is a deep-learning storm-event imagery dataset that spatially and temporally aligns GOES-16 ABI satellite imagery, NEXRAD radar mosaics and GOES-16 GLM lightning detections. Distributed through an AWS Open Data public S3 bucket, it supports precipitation nowcasting, synthetic radar, lightning and severe-convection tasks and is a key benchmark for geostationary infrared AI applications.
openness: open_no_restrictions
evidence: AWS Open Data Registry SEVIR page; MIT AI Accelerator SEVIR GitHub; NeurIPS 2020 SEVIR paper.
scale: about 1 TB according to SEVIR GitHub download instructions; event-based CONUS archive
feature_count: spatially and temporally aligned GOES-16 ABI, NEXRAD and GLM event samples
file_size: approximately 1 TB for full SEVIR download per GitHub documentation

name: Digital Typhoon Long-Term Himawari Infrared Tropical Cyclone Dataset
provider: National Institute of Informatics / Digital Typhoon / Himawari meteorological satellites / DIAS
theme_canonical: Benchmark / typhoon-centered infrared image time series
theme_raw: Digital Typhoon Dataset Version 1 and Version 2; typhoon-centered hourly meteorological satellite infrared channel images
coverage: Northern Hemisphere in Version 1; Northern and Southern Hemispheres in Version 2; tropical-cyclone-centered scenes
license: Digital Typhoon / NII / DIAS data terms; DOI record available through DIAS, access conditions should be checked before redistribution
formats: Typhoon-centered image datasets and associated software/metadata; converted brightness-temperature representations from Himawari IR imagery
access: Digital Typhoon dataset landing page and DIAS data repository DOI; Pyphoon software for ML research
doi_or_citation: DIAS DOI 10.20783/DIAS.664; Kitamoto et al. (2023) Digital Typhoon NeurIPS Datasets and Benchmarks; Kitamoto et al. (2024) arXiv:2411.16421 for Version 2
time_span: Himawari weather satellite infrared imagery since 1978; more than 40 years according to dataset page
notes: Accepted as a long-term Himawari infrared tropical-cyclone benchmark dataset. The NII landing page states that typhoon-centered images are created from hourly meteorological satellite infrared channel images, converted to brightness temperatures and calibrated across successive Himawari generations. The DIAS DOI timed out in lychee during v093, so the stable NII landing page is used as the row URL and DOI is recorded as citation evidence.
desc_zh: Digital Typhoon Dataset 是由 NII/Digital Typhoon 发布的台风中心红外影像数据集，基于 1978 年以来多代 Himawari 气象卫星的逐小时红外通道图像构建。数据被转换为亮温并跨传感器校准，形成 40 多年的统一时空台风影像资料，可用于热带气旋路径、强度、结构和机器学习建模。
desc_en: The Digital Typhoon Dataset is a typhoon-centered infrared image benchmark from NII/Digital Typhoon, built from hourly infrared-channel images from successive Himawari meteorological satellites since 1978. The imagery is converted to brightness temperature and calibrated across sensors, producing a more than 40-year spatiotemporal dataset for tropical-cyclone track, intensity, structure and machine-learning studies.
openness: open_landing_with_repository_terms
evidence: Digital Typhoon dataset landing page; DIAS DOI 10.20783/DIAS.664; NeurIPS 2023 and arXiv 2024 dataset papers.
scale: more than 40 years of hourly typhoon-centered Himawari infrared images
feature_count: Version 1 Northern Hemisphere; Version 2 Northern/Southern Hemispheres; sample counts depend on DIAS release
file_size: release-dependent; see DIAS repository

name: SatRain Satellite Precipitation Retrieval Benchmark Dataset
provider: International Precipitation Working Group / ipwgml / Colorado State University and collaborators
theme_canonical: Benchmark / geostationary infrared precipitation retrieval
theme_raw: SatRain benchmark dataset for satellite-based detection and estimation of rain, snow, graupel and hail
coverage: Training/validation/test splits over CONUS plus independent testing over Korea and Austria; global satellite input platforms are collocated around PMW overpasses
license: SatRain access package is MIT-licensed; dataset data terms follow the contributing satellite/radar/ancillary data sources and SatRain documentation
formats: Package-managed benchmark files for gridded and on-swath geometries; satellite observations, ancillary data and precipitation references
access: SatRain Python package and documentation automate data download; manual dataset download documented by SatRain; GitHub and Zenodo software releases provide access code
doi_or_citation: Pfreundschuh et al. (2025/2026) arXiv:2509.08816 / Nature Scientific Data; SatRain software DOI 10.5281/zenodo.17743454 and v1.0.1 DOI 10.5281/zenodo.18378554
time_span: Training data derived from 2018-2021 CONUS radar measurements according to article reference PDF; testing and independent regions are dataset-defined
notes: Accepted as an AI-ready precipitation benchmark dataset with explicit geostationary visible/infrared observations. The GitHub README states SatRain includes training, validation and test splits, collocated visible/infrared/microwave satellite observations, ancillary data and independent testing datasets. The documentation states that the package automates data download and loading, and the article data availability section says manual download is available from rain.atmos.colostate.edu/ipwgml; that manual directory was not included in formal lychee because it was slow during v093.
desc_zh: SatRain 是 IPWG 推出的卫星降水反演 AI benchmark，面向雨、雪、霰、冰雹的检测和定量估计。它把被动微波、地球静止可见光/红外观测、辅助环境变量与地面雷达/雨量计参考降水进行配准，提供标准训练、验证、测试和独立区域评估框架，适合发展基于红外和多传感器输入的下一代降水反演模型。
desc_en: SatRain is an IPWG AI benchmark for detecting and estimating rain, snow, graupel and hail from satellite observations. It collocates passive microwave data, geostationary visible/infrared observations, ancillary environmental variables and radar/gauge precipitation references into standardized training, validation, testing and independent evaluation splits, supporting next-generation infrared and multisensor precipitation retrieval models.
openness: open_code_and_documented_data_access
evidence: SatRain ReadTheDocs getting-started page; ipwgml/satrain GitHub README; arXiv:2509.08816; Zenodo software releases 17743454 and 18378554.
scale: multi-year benchmark with CONUS training and independent Korea/Austria testing; subset/full sizes controlled by package options
feature_count: collocated satellite observations, ancillary data and precipitation target/reference fields across benchmark splits
file_size: subset-dependent; full dataset selected with subset=xl in package download command

name: sub-SEVIR AI2ES Zarr Tutorial Benchmark Subset
provider: NSF AI2ES / Zenodo / SEVIR-derived GOES-16 ABI and NEXRAD subset
theme_canonical: Benchmark subset / severe storm imagery and lightning classification
theme_raw: sub-SEVIR; SEVIR tutorial subset; GOES-16 ABI and NEXRAD; Zarr
coverage: Continental United States; SEVIR-derived samples from 2017-2019
license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) according to Zenodo API record 7011372
formats: Zarr dataset packaged as sub-sevir.tar.gz on Zenodo
access: AI2ES dataset details page and Zenodo direct download
doi_or_citation: 10.5281/zenodo.7011372; AI2ES sub-SEVIR dataset details page
notes: Accepted as a distinct lightweight/tutorial benchmark subset of SEVIR rather than a duplicate of the full SEVIR S3 archive. AI2ES lists the data type as remote sensing, size 2 GB, file type Zarr, time resolution 5 minutes, grid 48 x 48, input data source GOES-16 ABI and NEXRAD, and the use case of determining what satellite/radar images contain lightning. Zenodo API lists DOI 10.5281/zenodo.7011372, CC-BY-4.0 and sub-sevir.tar.gz of about 1.97 GB.
desc_zh: sub-SEVIR 是 AI2ES 发布的 SEVIR 轻量教学/benchmark 子集，将 GOES-16 ABI 与 NEXRAD 数据整理为 Zarr 格式，便于快速训练和演示机器学习气象模型。该子集覆盖 2017-2019 年美国本土样本，5 分钟时间分辨率、48x48 网格，适合闪电相关分类、风暴影像建模和课程实验。
desc_en: sub-SEVIR is a lightweight AI2ES tutorial and benchmark subset derived from SEVIR, packaging GOES-16 ABI and NEXRAD data as Zarr for fast machine-learning experiments. It covers CONUS samples from 2017-2019 with 5-minute temporal resolution and 48x48 grids, supporting lightning-related classification, storm-imagery modeling and educational workflows.
openness: cc_by_4_0
evidence: AI2ES sub-SEVIR dataset details page; Zenodo API record 7011372 with DOI, CC-BY-4.0 license and file metadata.
scale: 2 GB tutorial subset; 48x48 grid; 5-minute time resolution
feature_count: one Zenodo tar.gz Zarr package plus tutorial link
file_size: 1,974,264,123 bytes in Zenodo API metadata

name: NOAA Global Mosaic of Geostationary Satellite Imagery GMGSI
provider: NOAA/NESDIS Office of Satellite and Product Operations / NOAA Open Data Dissemination / AWS Open Data
theme_canonical: Geostationary global infrared mosaic / cloud and temperature imagery
theme_raw: GMGSI; Global Mosaic of Geostationary Satellite Imagery; VIS/SIR/LIR/WV hourly mosaic
coverage: Global belt from about 60°N to 60°S composited from GOES East/West, Meteosat and Himawari geostationary satellites
license: NOAA Open Data Dissemination public data terms; NOAA requests attribution and states no endorsement by NOAA, NODD or AWS
formats: Public S3 object archive and imagery products; visible, shortwave infrared, longwave infrared and water-vapor channels
access: AWS Open Data Registry landing page and public S3 bucket s3://noaa-gmgsi-pds/; no AWS account required for download
doi_or_citation: NOAA GMGSI AWS Open Data Registry; NOAA OSPO Global Mosaic of Geostationary Satellite Imagery product page
time_span: Operational near-real-time hourly updates; AWS registry describes updates as hourly
notes: Accepted as a distinct open global geostationary infrared mosaic product. AWS registry states GMGSI includes VIS, shortwave infrared, longwave infrared and water-vapor imagery composited from GOES East/West, Meteosat-10/9 and Himawari-9, with approximate 8 km spatial resolution and hourly updates. NOAA OSPO describes global 60N-60S coverage using five geostationary satellites and four spectral bands: longwave infrared, shortwave infrared, mid-wave infrared/water vapor and visible.
desc_zh: NOAA/NESDIS GMGSI 将多颗静止气象卫星的可见光、短波红外、长波红外和水汽通道拼接为全球 60°N-60°S 小时级马赛克。长波红外可反映云覆盖和陆海温度格局，短波红外有助于夜间云/雾识别，适合全球天气监测和云场背景分析。
desc_en: NOAA/NESDIS GMGSI composites visible, shortwave infrared, longwave infrared and water-vapor imagery from multiple geostationary weather satellites into an hourly global mosaic from roughly 60°N to 60°S. Its longwave infrared channel supports cloud and land/sea temperature interpretation, while shortwave infrared helps nighttime cloud and fog analysis.
access_status: Public AWS Open Data bucket and NOAA product documentation are reachable; individual object inventory should be inspected for exact file naming and retention windows
openness: open
evidence: AWS registry: GMGSI public S3 bucket, no AWS account required, VIS/SIR/LIR/WV bands, hourly updates and NOAA NODD open data terms; NOAA OSPO: 60N-60S global product with four spectral bands from five geostationary satellites
scale: global geostationary mosaic, approximately 8 km and hourly according to AWS registry
feature_count: VIS, SIR, LIR and WV hourly mosaic channels
file_size: not summarized on registry landing page; public S3 archive size should be calculated from bucket inventory if needed

name: NASA GeoNEX Level 1G TOA Reflectance and Brightness Temperature Products
provider: NASA Earth Exchange GeoNEX / GOES ABI / Himawari AHI / geostationary sensor partners
theme_canonical: Gridded geostationary top-of-atmosphere brightness temperature
theme_raw: GeoNEX Level 1G TOA Reflectance Brightness Temperature; GOES-16 ABI; GOES-17 ABI; Himawari-8 AHI
coverage: GOES ABI and Himawari AHI domains including Americas and Asia-Pacific; NASA product page lists gridded domains from 60°N to 60°S for each satellite sector
license: NASA Earth Exchange open-science data portal terms; some full data access paths may require NASA Advanced Supercomputing or portal account access
formats: Geographic tiled grid products; 0.01 degree visible/NIR and 0.02 degree shortwave/thermal infrared grids; TOA BRF and emissive-band brightness temperature
access: NASA GeoNEX data-products page and NASA Earth Exchange data portal; NAS users can access products under /nex/datapool/geonex/
doi_or_citation: NASA GeoNEX data-products page; Wang et al. GeoNEX land product references listed by NASA
time_span: Product- and satellite-dependent; NASA page documents GOES-16/17 ABI and Himawari-8 AHI product families
notes: Accepted as the canonical NASA GeoNEX infrared-capable L1G product family. The NASA page states Level 1G TOA products provide radiometrically calibrated BRFs for reflective bands, brightness temperatures for emissive bands and Sun angles registered to a common geographic tiled grid. It lists 0.02 degree shortwave/thermal infrared grids and full-domain temporal resolution of 15 minutes, with 5 minute CONUS resolution for GOES ABI.
desc_zh: NASA GeoNEX Level 1G TOA 产品将 GOES ABI、Himawari AHI 等新一代静止卫星观测统一配准到地理瓦片网格，提供反射波段 TOA 反射率和发射波段亮温。短波/热红外网格约 0.02°，全域 15 分钟、GOES CONUS 可达 5 分钟，适合陆面、云、温度和高频时序研究。
desc_en: NASA GeoNEX Level 1G TOA products register observations from GOES ABI, Himawari AHI and related geostationary sensors onto common geographic tiles, providing TOA reflectance for reflective bands and brightness temperature for emissive bands. The shortwave and thermal infrared grids are documented at about 0.02 degrees, with 15-minute full-domain cadence and 5-minute GOES CONUS cadence.
access_status: Public documentation is reachable; product download may use the NASA Earth Exchange portal and NAS account paths for complete holdings
openness: open-with-registration-or-portal
evidence: NASA GeoNEX data-products table lists TOA Reflectance Brightness Temperature and LST products; Level 1G section explicitly includes emissive-band brightness temperatures and shortwave/thermal infrared grids at 0.02 degrees
scale: continental to hemispheric geostationary sectors, tiled 0.02 degree SWIR/TIR grids with 15 minute or 5 minute cadence
feature_count: Level 1G TOA reflective-band BRF, emissive-band brightness temperature and Sun/view geometry products
file_size: not summarized on public landing page; varies by satellite, tile and time period

name: NASA GeoNEX Coincident Ground Observations GeCGO Dataset
provider: NASA GeoNEX / ESSD GeCGO authors / AmeriFlux and partner site networks
theme_canonical: Tower-site subsets of geostationary infrared and land products
theme_raw: GeCGO; GeoNEX Coincident Ground Observations; ABI radiometric temperature; LST; CSV/JSON site subsets
coverage: 1,586 field and flux-tower sites across the Americas, spanning 14 ground networks according to the ESSD 2026 dataset paper
license: Copernicus ESSD open-access article terms and NASA GeoNEX data portal access terms; dataset files are served through NASA GeoNEX GeCGO portal
formats: CSV and JSON site-centered subsets; paper describes 17x17, 9x9 and 5x5 grids depending on product spatial resolution
access: ESSD dataset paper and NASA GeoNEX GeCGO data portal at https://data.nas.nasa.gov/gecgo/data.php
doi_or_citation: https://doi.org/10.5194/essd-18-397-2026; NASA GeoNEX GeCGO data portal
time_span: Product- and site-dependent; ESSD paper compares geostationary records with ground-site observations for high-frequency land-atmosphere analysis
notes: Accepted as a literature-documented open GeoNEX derivative containing infrared-relevant station subsets. ESSD 2026 states GeCGO extracts GeoNEX observations over 1,586 field/flux tower sites, provides CSV and JSON outputs through the NASA GeoNEX data portal, and includes examples such as hourly LST and ABI band 14 radiometric temperature subset data. A direct lychee probe of the GeCGO portal was slow in this run, so the verified article URL is used as canonical and the portal is retained as access evidence.
desc_zh: GeCGO 从 NASA GeoNEX 中提取 1,586 个地面观测站和通量塔周边的静止卫星观测子集，并以 CSV/JSON 形式发布。论文示例包含 LST 和 GOES ABI band 14 radiometric temperature 等红外相关变量，可与 AmeriFlux 等半小时地面观测直接配对，用于陆气过程和模型评估。
desc_en: GeCGO extracts station-centered GeoNEX observations around 1,586 field and flux-tower sites and distributes them as CSV/JSON subsets. The ESSD paper documents infrared-relevant examples including LST and GOES ABI band 14 radiometric temperature, enabling direct comparison with half-hourly AmeriFlux and related ground observations.
access_status: Peer-reviewed ESSD landing page is reachable; NASA GeCGO portal URL is documented by the article but should be rechecked separately for interactive availability
openness: open-with-portal
evidence: ESSD 2026 article: data available at NASA GeoNEX GeCGO portal; 1,586 sites across 14 networks; CSV/JSON outputs; examples include LST hourly and ABI band 14 radiometric temperature subset data
scale: site-centered subsets over 1,586 tower/field sites across the Americas
feature_count: 1,586 sites and multiple GeoNEX product subsets including infrared temperature variables
file_size: not summarized in the article landing page; varies by selected site/product/time range

desc_en: The Global Mosaic of Geostationary Images Zarr/Icechunk Archive repackages multi-satellite global geostationary imagery mosaics into cloud-native Zarr v3 and Icechunk stores for array-oriented analytics and machine-learning workflows. It acts as an analysis-ready access layer for GMGSI-like hourly geostationary mosaics.

notes: Accepted as a geostationary infrared/water-vapor derived atmospheric product. EUMETSAT API states GII uses an optimal estimation scheme to fit clear-sky vertical temperature and humidity profiles constrained by NWP forecasts to FCI observations in WV6.3, WV7.3, IR8.7, IR9.7, IR10.5, IR12.3 and IR13.3. Retrieved profiles are used to compute Lifted Index, K Index, Layer Precipitable Water and Total Precipitable Water. Product is netCDF, every 10 minutes, 3x3 IR pixels, about 6 km at nadir.

desc_en: The EUMETSAT MTG FCI Global Instability Indices 0 Degree product uses FCI water-vapor and infrared observations, constrained by NWP forecasts, to retrieve clear-sky temperature and humidity profiles and compute Lifted Index, K Index, Layer Precipitable Water and Total Precipitable Water. It is a 10-minute netCDF product at about 6 km nadir resolution for convective-potential and nowcasting applications.

time_span: 2020-09-01 onward according to EUMETSAT browse API date field
notes: Accepted as an operational RGB imagery product with explicit infrared-channel inputs, not as a quantitative cloud microphysics retrieval. EUMETSAT API states the Day Microphysics RGB uses VIS0.8 reflectance in red as a proxy for cloud optical depth and water/ice amount, IR3.9 solar reflectance in green as a qualitative measure of cloud particle size and phase, and IR10.8 brightness temperature to modulate blue. The colour scheme is useful for cloud analysis, convection, fog, snow and fires.
desc_zh: EUMETSAT MSG Day Microphysics RGB 0 Degree Product 是基于 MSG/SEVIRI 的日间云微物理 RGB 合成产品。EUMETSAT 说明红色通道使用 VIS0.8 反射率，绿色使用 IR3.9 太阳反射率作为云粒子大小和相态的定性指标，蓝色由 IR10.8 亮温调制，适合云分析、对流、雾、雪和火点识别。
desc_en: The EUMETSAT MSG Day Microphysics RGB 0 Degree Product is a SEVIRI RGB composite for daytime cloud microphysics interpretation. EUMETSAT documents VIS0.8 reflectance in red, IR3.9 solar reflectance in green as a qualitative indicator of cloud particle size and phase, and IR10.8 brightness temperature modulating blue, supporting cloud, convection, fog, snow and fire analysis.

desc_en: The EUMETSAT MSG Convection RGB 0 Degree Product is an operational RGB for severe-convection interpretation. It combines WV6.2-WV7.3 brightness-temperature difference in red, IR3.9-IR10.8 brightness-temperature difference in green, and NIR1.6-VIS0.6 reflectance difference in blue; EUMETSAT notes bright-yellow signatures for overshooting convective clouds with small ice particles at cloud tops.

time_span: 2025-05-01 onward according to EUMETSAT browse API date field
notes: Accepted as an MTG/FCI RGB product explicitly derived from infrared wavelength differences for fog and low-cloud detection. EUMETSAT API explains that low clouds and fog can be difficult in individual IR channels, and that the Night Microphysics RGB uses three IR wavelengths. Low water clouds and fog emit less radiation at 3.8 µm than at 10.5 µm, and the brightness-temperature-difference field is used in the green channel to highlight low clouds.
desc_zh: EUMETSAT MTG Fog and Low Clouds RGB 0 Degree Product 是 MTG/FCI 的夜间微物理 RGB 产品，用于雾和低云识别。EUMETSAT 说明该产品使用三个红外波长，利用 3.8 µm 与 10.5 µm 之间的亮温差突出低水云和雾，弥补单一红外通道对低云/雾温差不明显的问题。
desc_en: The EUMETSAT MTG Fog and Low Clouds RGB 0 Degree Product is an MTG/FCI Night Microphysics RGB for fog and low-cloud detection. EUMETSAT explains that it uses three infrared wavelengths, including the 3.8 µm versus 10.5 µm brightness-temperature difference, to highlight low water clouds and fog that are difficult to distinguish in individual IR channels.

notes: Accepted as a long-term geostationary AMV Thematic Climate Data Record with explicit water-vapor and infrared channel evidence. EUMETSAT API states Release 2 contains AMVs at all heights below the tropopause, derived from Water Vapour 6.2 and Infrared 10.8 images from MVIRI on Meteosat First Generation and SEVIRI on Meteosat Second Generation. Vectors are retrieved by tracking clouds and water-vapor patterns; height assignment uses the Cross-Correlation Contribution function and vectors are estimated by averaging speed and height over four consecutive images. Products are netCDF4 and cover Meteosat-2 to Meteosat-11 from 1981 to 2019.

desc_en: The EUMETSAT Atmospheric Motion Vectors Climate Data Record Release 2 is a long-term AMV thematic climate data record from Meteosat First Generation MVIRI and MSG SEVIRI, spanning September 1981 to August 2019. It uses WV6.2 and IR10.8 imagery to track cloud and water-vapor patterns and retrieve wind vectors below the tropopause, distributed as netCDF4.

notes: Accepted from the official EUMETSAT Data Store as a distinct operational collection not present in v104. Infrared relevance is through the MSG/SEVIRI rapid-scan cloud-processing chain; the product is a cloud scene classification derived from geostationary imager observations and used as a basis for other cloud products. API abstract summary: The Rapid Scanning Services (RSS) Cloud Mask product describes the scene type (either 'clear' or 'cloudy') on a pixel level. Each pixel is classified as one of the following four types: clear sky over water, clear sky over land, cloud, or not processed (off Earth disc). desc_en: The EUMETSAT MSG Rapid Scan Cloud Mask is a pixel-level cloud mask product for the Rapid Scanning Service, available from October 2008 onward. It classifies pixels as clear water, clear land, cloud or not processed, supporting nowcasting and downstream cloud, land-surface and ocean-surface remote-sensing workflows.

doi_or_citation: EO:EUM:DAT:MSG:HRSEVIRI-IODC
notes:
Accepted from the official EUMETSAT Data Store as a distinct operational collection not present in v104. The API abstract states that the collection contains rectified Meteosat SEVIRI image data transmitted as High Rate data in 12 spectral channels, with radiometric and geometric quality control information. API abstract summary: Rectified (level 1.5) Meteosat SEVIRI image data. The data is transmitted as High Rate transmissions in 12 spectral channels. Level 1.5 image data corresponds to the geolocated and radiometrically pre-processed image data, ready for further processing, e.g. the extraction of meteorological products. Any spacecraft specific effects have been removed, and in particular, linearisation and equalisation of the image radiometry has been performed for all SEVIRI channels. The on-board blackbody data has been processed. Both radiometric and geometric quality control information is included.
Images are made available with different timeliness according to the latency: quarter-hourly images with a latency of more than 3 hours and hourly images if latency is less than 3 hours (for a total of 87 images per day).
To enhance the perception for areas which are on the night side of the Earth a different mapping with increased contrast is applied for IR3.9 product. The greyscale mapping is based on the EBBT which allows to map the ranges 200 K to 300 K for the night and 250 K to 330 K for the day.

From 1 June 2022, Meteosat-9 at 45.5° E is the prime satellite for the IODC service, replacing Meteosat-8 (located at 41.5° E while in operation).

A selection of single channel data are visualised in our EUMETView service.
desc_zh: EUMETSAT MSG High Rate SEVIRI Level 1.5 Image Data Indian Ocean 是印度洋覆盖区的 MSG/SEVIRI 1.5 级定位和辐射预处理影像集合，自 2017 年 2 月起提供。SEVIRI 的 12 个光谱通道包含多条红外和水汽通道，数据可用于气象产品提取、云分析、海陆表面和夜间场景监测。
desc_en: The EUMETSAT MSG High Rate SEVIRI Level 1.5 Image Data Indian Ocean collection provides geolocated and radiometrically pre-processed MSG/SEVIRI imagery for the Indian Ocean coverage service from February 2017 onward. SEVIRI has 12 spectral channels including infrared and water-vapor channels, supporting meteorological product extraction, cloud analysis and land/ocean monitoring.

doi_or_citation: EO:EUM:DAT:MSG:MSG15-RSS
notes: Accepted from the official EUMETSAT Data Store as a distinct operational collection not present in v104. The API abstract documents rectified Meteosat SEVIRI Rapid Scan image data, 5-minute scan cycles, channel 1-11 segment dissemination and HRV segments, with calibration as in full Earth scan. API abstract summary: Rectified (level 1.5) Meteosat SEVIRI Rapid Scan image data. The baseline scan region is a reduced area of the top 1/3 of a nominal repeat cycle, covering a latitude range from approximately 15 degrees to 70 degrees. The service generates repeat cycles at 5-minute intervals (the same as currently used for weather radars). The dissemination of RSS data is similar to the normal dissemination, with image segments based on 464 lines and compatible with the full disk level 1.5 data scans. Epilogue and prologue (L1.5 Header and L1.5 Trailer) have the same structure. Calibration is as in Full Earth Scan. Image rectification is to 9.5 degreesE. The scans start at 00:00, 00:05, 00:10, 00:15 ... etc. (5 min scan). The differences from the nominal Full Earth scan are that for channels 1 - 11, only segments 6 - 8 are disseminated and for the High Resolution Visible Channel only segments 16 - 24 are disseminated. A selection of single channel data are visualised in our EUMETView service.
desc_zh: EUMETSAT MSG Rapid Scan High Rate SEVIRI Level 1.5 Image Data 是 MSG 快速扫描服务的 1.5 级影像集合，自 2008 年 5 月起以约 5 分钟周期覆盖标称全盘扫描上部三分之一区域。它保留 SEVIRI 1-11 通道和高分辨率可见光通道的快速扫描片段，其中包含红外和水汽通道，适合临近预报和快速演变天气过程分析。
desc_en: The EUMETSAT MSG Rapid Scan High Rate SEVIRI Level 1.5 Image Data collection provides rectified rapid-scan MSG/SEVIRI imagery from May 2008 onward, with a five-minute repeat cycle over the northern part of the nominal disk. It includes SEVIRI channels 1-11 and HRV rapid-scan segments, covering infrared and water-vapor channels for nowcasting and fast-evolving weather analysis.

The API abstract explicitly states that HIRS operates with 20 channels, 19 infrared and one visible, and that this collection includes operational-mode data from Metop-B. API abstract summary: This is the second release of the reprocessed polar Atmospheric Motion Vectors (AMV) Thematic Climate Data Record (TCDR) from the Advanced Very High Resolution Radiometer (AVHRR) in Global Area Coverage (GAC), from TIROS-N, NOAA-06, 07, 08, 09, 10, 11, 12, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18 and 19 and Metop-A and -B. It contains AMVs at all heights below the tropopause, derived from images in the Infrared channel at 10.8 microns. Vectors are retrieved by tracking the motion of clouds in two consecutive images. The height assignment of the AMVs is calculated using the Cross-Correlation Contribution (CCC) function to determine the height using the pixels that contribute the most to the vectors. A quality indicator is derived for each vector to assess the reliability of the retrieval. Products are stored in netCDF4 format and cover the period from January 1979 to September 2019.

This is a Thematic Climate Data Record (TCDR).

doi_or_citation: EO:EUM:DAT:0758
notes:
Accepted from official EUMETSAT Data Store collection metadata in v106. Infrared relevance is through IASI Level 1C radiance spectra and principal-component compression of the hyperspectral infrared sounder bands. API abstract summary: This is release 1 of the IASI Principal Component Scores (PCS) Fundamental Data Record (FDR) from Metop-A and -B. Principal component compression (PCC) is applied to the IASI Level 1c radiance spectra. PCC works by representing multidimensional data, like IASI spectra, in a lower dimensional space, which accounts for most of the variance seen in the data. The PCC is applied individually to each of the three IASI spectral bands and reduces the size of the measurement files of up to a factor of 50. With the use of Eigenvector files the spectra can be reconstructed from the PCS files for downstream applications.
On the 30 August 2023, year 2022 was added to the FDR. On the 20 January 2025, year 2023 was added to the FDR. On the 21 November 2025, year 2024 was added to the FDR.
desc_zh: EUMETSAT IASI Principal Components Scores FDR Release 1 是 Metop-A/B IASI Level 1C 辐射光谱的主成分得分基础数据记录，覆盖 2007 年 7 月至 2024 年 12 月。该产品对 IASI 三个光谱波段分别进行主成分压缩，可用特征向量文件重建光谱，减少数据体积并服务下游红外声探测应用。
desc_en: The EUMETSAT IASI Principal Components Scores FDR Release 1 is a fundamental data record of principal-component scores from Metop-A/B IASI Level 1C radiance spectra, spanning July 2007 to December 2024. Principal-component compression is applied to each IASI spectral band, allowing spectra to be reconstructed with eigenvector files for downstream infrared sounding applications.

notes: Accepted from official EUMETSAT Data Store collection metadata in v106. Distinct from the existing netCDF ASR row; this row captures the official BUFR distribution. API abstract summary: The All-Sky Radiance (ASR) product is a segmented product that provides FCI Level 1C data statistics within processing segments referred to as Field-of-Regard (FoR). The statistics are computed on the L1C radiances (for all FCI channels), brightness temperatures (for the eight IR channels) and reflectances (for the eight visible and near-infrared channels) and include the mean value, standard deviation, minimum and maximum values within the FoR. The ASR product is available in BUFR and netCDF format, every 10 minutes, at a spatial resolution of 16x16 pixels (IR) and 32x32 pixels (VIS).

API abstract summary: The Atmospheric Motion Vector (AMV) product is realised by tracking clouds or water vapour features in consecutive FCI satellite images based on feature tracking between each pair of consecutive repeat cycles, leading to two intermediate AMV products for an image triplet. The final product is then derived from these two intermediate products, and includes information on wind speed, direction, height, and quality. AMVs are extracted from the FCI VIS 0.8, IR 3.8 (night only), IR 10.5, WV 6.3 and WV 7.3 channels. The AMV product is available in BUFR and netCDF format, every 30 minutes.

notes: Accepted from official EUMETSAT Data Store collection metadata in v106. Infrared relevance is explicit through MVIRI Water Vapor 6.2 and Infrared 10.8 channels. API abstract summary: This is the first release of the reprocessed Meteosat First Generation (MFG) Atmospheric Motion Vectors (AMV) Thematic Climate Data Record (TCDR) for the Indian Ocean Data Coverage (IODC) region. It contains AMV at all heights below the tropopause, derived from images in 2 channels (Water Vapor 6.2, Infrared 10.8) of the instrument MVIRI on board MFG. Vectors are retrieved by tracking the motion of clouds and other atmospheric constituents such as water vapor patterns. The height assignment of the AMVs is calculated using the Cross-Correlation Contribution (CCC) function to determine the height using the pixels that contribute the most to the vectors. The final vector is estimated averaging the speed and height over 4 consecutive images. A quality indicator is derived for each vector to assess the reliability of the retrieval. Products are stored in netCDF4 format and generated fromMeteosat-7 satellite, covering the period from November 2006 to March 2017.

notes:
Accepted from official EUMETSAT Data Store collection metadata in v106. Infrared relevance is explicit through AVHRR/3 infrared channel 4 imagery. API abstract summary: Tropospheric winds derived globally using the tandem configuration of METOP-A and METOP-B AVHRR instruments. Two complementary products are generated considering either Metop-A or Metop-B as the reference platform.

This is the first release of the global atmospheric wind vectors data record. Data are accessible from the EUMETSAT Data Centre. This uses the version 3.1.1 of the EUMETSAT operational algorithm and forecast from ERA-Interim reanalyses as auxiliary data. The data record includes products generated using IR images (channel 4) taken from two AVHRR/3 imager on-board the Metop-A and Metop-B polar satellites. Each product contains several wind vectors with their associated speed, direction, height, and several quality indicators. The dataset covers the period from the 24 April 2013 until December 2017.

The coverage is global. The dataset is available in two formats: the EUMETSAT EPS native format and the BUFR format.

Atmospheric motion vectors (AMVs) are a valuable observation type for providing dynamical information for forecast models.

This is a Thematic Climate Data Record (TCDR) produced for the Copernicus Climate Change Service.

The data record includes products generated using IR images (channel 4) taken from two AVHRR/3 imager on-board the Metop-A and Metop-B polar satellites. Each product contains several wind vectors with their associated speed, direction, height, and several quality indicators. The dataset covers the period from the 24 April 2013 until December 2017.

The coverage is global. The dataset is available in two formats: the EUMETSAT EPS native format and the BUFR format.

Atmospheric motion vectors (AMVs) are a valuable observation type for providing dynamical information for forecast models.

This is a Thematic Climate Data Record (TCDR) produced for the Copernicus Climate Change Service.
scale: global polar-orbiting AVHRR AMV data record

notes: Accepted from NASA CMR official collection metadata in v108. The CMR summary identifies this as a NOAA-21 Visible Infrared Imaging Radiometer Suite land surface temperature and emissivity product and describes cloud-free LST averaging or compositing from VJ221/VJ221A1 inputs. The NOAA-21 Visible Infrared Imaging Radiometer Suite (VIIRS) Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity (LST&E) Day Version 2 product (VJ221A1D) is compiled daily from daytime Level 2 Gridded (L2G) intermediate products. The L2G process maps the daily ([VJ221](https://doi.org/10.5067/VIIRS/VJ221.002)) swath granules onto a sinusoidal MODIS grid and stores all observations overlapping a gridded cell for a given day. The VJ221A1 algorithm sorts through all these observations for each cell and estimates the final LST value as an average from all cloud-free observations that have good LST accuracies. The daytime average is weighted by the observation coverage for that cell. Only observations having observation coverage more than a certain threshold (15%) are considered for this averaging. The 1 kilometer dataset is derived through resampling the native 750 meter VIIRS resolution in the input product. The VJ221A1D product is developed synergistically with the Moderate Resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer (MODIS) LST&E Version 6.1 product ([MOD21A1D](https://doi.org/10.5067/MODIS/MOD21A1D.061)) using the same input atmospheric products and algorithmic approach. The overall objective for NASA VIIRS products is to ensure the algorithms and products are compatible with the MODIS Terra and Aqua algorithms to promote the continuity of the Earth Observation System (EOS) mission. Additional details regarding the method used to create this Level 3 (L3) product are available in the Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document (ATBD). The VJ221A1D product contains seven variables: LST, quality control, emissivity for bands M14, M15, and M16, view zenith angle, and time of observation. A low-resolution browse image for LST is also available for each VJ221A1D granule. Known Issues * For complete information about known issues please refer to the [MODIS/VIIRS Land Quality Assessment website](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/knownissue?sensor=VIIRS&sat=J2&as=5200) and the User Guide.

notes: Accepted from NASA CMR official collection metadata in v108. The CMR summary identifies this as a NOAA-21 Visible Infrared Imaging Radiometer Suite land surface temperature and emissivity product and describes cloud-free LST averaging or compositing from VJ221/VJ221A1 inputs. The NOAA-21 Visible Infrared Imaging Radiometer Suite (VIIRS) Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity (LST&E) 8-day product (VJ221A2) combines the daily ([VJ221A1D](http://doi.org/10.5067/VIIRS/VJ221A1D.002)) and ([VJ221A1N](http://doi.org/10.5067/VIIRS/VJ221A1N.002)) products over an 8-day compositing period into a single product. The VJ221A2 dataset is an 8-day composite LST&E product at 1 kilometer resolution that uses an algorithm based on a simple-averaging method. The algorithm calculates the average from all the cloud-free VJ221A1D and VJ221A1N daily acquisitions from the 8-day period. Unlike the VJ221A1 datasets where the daytime and nighttime acquisitions are separate products, the VJ221A2 contains both daytime and nighttime acquisitions as separate science dataset (SDS) variables within a single Hierarchical Data Format (HDF) file. The VJ221A2 product is developed synergistically with the Moderate Resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer (MODIS) LST&E Version 6.1 product ([MOD21A2](https://doi.org/10.5067/MODIS/MOD21A2.061)) using the same input atmospheric products and algorithmic approach. The overall objective for NASA VIIRS products is to ensure the algorithms and products are compatible with the MODIS Terra and Aqua algorithms to promote the continuity of the Earth Observation System (EOS) mission. Additional details regarding the method used to create this Level 3 (L3) product are available in the Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document (ATBD). The VJ221A2 product contains 11 variables: LST, quality control, view zenith angle, and time of observation for both day and night observations along with emissivity for bands M14, M15, and M16. Low-resolution browse images for day and night LST are also available for each VJ221A2 granule. Known Issues * For complete information about known issues please refer to the [MODIS/VIIRS Land Quality Assessment website](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/knownissue?sensor=VIIRS&sat=J2&as=5200) and the User Guide.

evidence: NASA CMR concept C2832144235-LPCLOUD returned short_name VJ221C2, version 002, title 'VIIRS/JPSS2 Land Surface Temperature/Emissivity 8-Day L3 Global 0.05Deg CMG V002', data_center LPCLOUD, time_start 2023-02-10T00:00:00.000Z, DOI https://doi.org/10.5067/VIIRS/VJ221C2.002, Earthdata catalog URL https://www.earthdata.nasa.gov/data/catalog/lpcloud-vj221c2-002, and summary: The NOAA-21 Visible Infrared Imaging Radiometer Suite (VIIRS) Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity (LST&E) 8-day Climate Modeling Grid Version 2 product (VJ221C2) combines the daily ([VJ221A1D](http://doi.org/10.5067/VIIRS/VJ221A1D.002)) and ([VJ221A1N](http://doi.org/10.5067/VIIRS/VJ221A1N.002)) products over an 8-day compositing period into a single product. The VJ221C2 dataset is an 8-day composite LST&E product at 0.05 degree (~5,600 meter) resolution that uses an algorithm based on a simple-averaging method and is formatted as a CMG for use in climate simulation models. The algorithm calculates the average from all the cloud-free VJ221A1D and VJ221A1N daily acquisitions from the 8-day period. Unlike the VJ221A1 datasets where the daytime and nighttime acquisitions are separate products, the VJ221C2 contains both daytime and nighttime acquisitions as separate science dataset (SDS) variables within a single Hierarchical Data Format (HDF) file. The overall objective for NASA VIIRS products is to ensure the algorithms and products are compatible with the MODIS Terra and Aqua algorithms to promote the continuity of the Earth Observation System (EOS) mission. Additional details regarding the method used to create this Level 3 (L3) product are available in the Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document (ATBD). The VJ121C2 product contains 27 variables: LST, quality control, view zenith angle, and time of observation for both day and night observations along with emissivity for bands M14, M15, and M16. Low-resolution browse images for day and night LST are also available for each VJ121C2 granule. Known Issues: • For complete information about known issues please refer to the [MODIS/VIIRS Land Quality Assessment website](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/knownissue?sensor=VIIRS&sat=J2&as=5200).

doi_or_citation: https://doi.org/10.5067/MODIS/MOD21.061; CMR C2565791036-LPCLOUD; Earthdata Search https://search.earthdata.nasa.gov/search/granules?p=C2565791036-LPCLOUD
time_span: See CMR collection metadata for temporal extent
notes: Accepted from NASA CMR official collection metadata in v110. CMR summary states the MOD21 TES algorithm dynamically retrieves LST and spectral emissivity from MODIS thermal infrared bands 29, 31 and 32 with water-vapor-scaling atmospheric correction. The MOD21 Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity (LST&E) swath data product is produced daily in five minute temporal increments of satellite acquisition. The swath is approximately 2,030 pixels along track and 1,354 pixels per line, at a nadir resolution of 1,000 meters. The MOD21 Land Surface Temperature (LST) algorithm differs from the [MOD11](https://doi.org/10.5067/modis/mod11_l2.061) algorithm in that the MOD21 LST algorithm is based on the ASTER Temperature/Emissivity Separation (TES) technique, whereas the MOD11 uses the split-window technique. The MOD21 TES algorithm uses a physics-based algorithm to dynamically retrieve both LST and spectral emissivity simultaneously from the MODIS thermal infrared bands 29, 31, and 32. The TES algorithm is combined with an improved Water Vapor Scaling (WVS) atmospheric correction scheme to stabilize the retrieval during very warm and humid conditions. Additional details regarding the method used to create this Level 2 (L2) product are available in the Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document (ATBD). Known Issues * Users of MODIS LST products may notice an increase in occurrences of [extreme high temperature outliers](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/displayissue?id=117) in the unfiltered MxD21 Version 6 and 6.1 products compared to the heritage MxD11 LST products. This can occur especially over desert regions like the Sahara where undetected cloud and dust can negatively impact both the MxD21 and MxD11 retrieval algorithms. * In the MxD11 LST products, these contaminated pixels are flagged in the algorithm and set to fill values in the output products based on differences in the band 32 and band 31 radiances used in the generalized split window algorithm. In the MxD21 LST products, values for the contaminated pixels are retained in the output products (and may result in overestimated temperatures), and users need to apply Quality Control (QC) filtering and other error analyses for filtering out bad values. High temperature outlier thresholds are not employed in MxD21 since it would potentially remove naturally occurring hot surface targets such as fires and lava flows. * High atmospheric aerosol optical depth (AOD) caused by vast dust outbreaks in the Sahara and other deserts highlighted in the example documentation are the primary reason for high outlier surface temperature values (and corresponding low emissivity values) in the MxD21 LST products. Future versions of the MxD21 product will include a dust flag from the MODIS aerosol product and/or brightness temperature look up tables to filter out contaminated dust pixels. It should be noted that in the MxD11B day/night algorithm products, more advanced cloud filtering is employed in the multi-day products based on a temporal analysis of historical LST over cloudy areas. This may result in more stringent filtering of dust contaminated pixels in these products. * In order to mitigate the impact of dust in the MxD21 V6 and 6.1 products, the science team recommends using a combination of the existing QC bits, emissivity values, and estimated product errors, to confidently remove bad pixels from analysis. For more details, refer to this dust and cloud contamination [example documentation](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/data/userguide/MOD21_dust_QC_examples.pdf). * For complete information about known issues please refer to the [MODIS/VIIRS Land Quality Assessment website](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/knownissue?sensor=MODIS&sat=Terra&as=61).
desc_zh: NASA MODIS/Terra Land Surface Temperature/3-Band Emissivity 5-Min L2 1km V061 是 Terra/MODIS Collection 6.1 陆表温度与三波段发射率产品。该 L2 5 分钟 swath 产品以约 1 km 天底分辨率提供每日轨道级 LST&E，是 Terra MOD21 具体产品族的基础层级。该产品族使用 ASTER TES 思路，从 MODIS 热红外 29、31 和 32 波段同时反演 LST 与谱发射率。
desc_en: NASA MODIS/Terra Land Surface Temperature/3-Band Emissivity 5-Min L2 1km V061 is a Terra/MODIS Collection 6.1 Land Surface Temperature and 3-Band Emissivity product. The Level 2 five-minute swath product provides daily orbit-level LST&E at about 1 km nadir resolution and is the base Terra MOD21 product level. The product family uses the ASTER TES approach to retrieve LST and spectral emissivity simultaneously from MODIS thermal-infrared bands 29, 31 and 32.

The MOD21A2 dataset is an 8-day composite LST product at 1,000 meter spatial resolution that uses an algorithm based on a simple averaging method. The algorithm calculates the average from all the cloud free [MOD21A1D](http://doi.org/10.5067/MODIS/MOD21A1D.061) and [MOD21A1N](http://doi.org/10.5067/MODIS/MOD21A1N.061) daily acquisitions from the 8-day period. Unlike the MOD21A1 data sets where the daytime and nighttime acquisitions are separate products, the MOD21A2 contains both daytime and nighttime acquisitions as separate Science Dataset (SDS) layers within a single Hierarchical Data Format (HDF) file. The LST, Quality Control (QC), view zenith angle, and viewing time have separate day and night SDS layers, while the values for the MODIS emissivity bands 29, 31, and 32 are the average of both the nighttime and daytime acquisitions. Additional details regarding the method used to create this Level 3 (L3) product are available in the Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document (ATBD). Known Issues * Users of MODIS LST products may notice an increase in occurrences of [extreme high temperature outliers](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/displayissue?id=117) in the unfiltered MxD21 Version 6 and 6.1 products compared to the heritage MxD11 LST products. This can occur especially over desert regions like the Sahara where undetected cloud and dust can negatively impact both the MxD21 and MxD11 retrieval algorithms. * In the MxD11 LST products, these contaminated pixels are flagged in the algorithm and set to fill values in the output products based on differences in the band 32 and band 31 radiances used in the generalized split window algorithm. In the MxD21 LST products, values for the contaminated pixels are retained in the output products (and may result in overestimated temperatures), and users need to apply Quality Control (QC) filtering and other error analyses for filtering out bad values. High temperature outlier thresholds are not employed in MxD21 since it would potentially remove naturally occurring hot surface targets such as fires and lava flows. * High atmospheric aerosol optical depth (AOD) caused by vast dust outbreaks in the Sahara and other deserts highlighted in the example documentation are the primary reason for high outlier surface temperature values (and corresponding low emissivity values) in the MxD21 LST products. Future versions of the MxD21 product will include a dust flag from the MODIS aerosol product and/or brightness temperature look up tables to filter out contaminated dust pixels. It should be noted that in the MxD11B day/night algorithm products, more advanced cloud filtering is employed in the multi-day products based on a temporal analysis of historical LST over cloudy areas. This may result in more stringent filtering of dust contaminated pixels in these products. * In order to mitigate the impact of dust in the MxD21 V6 and 6.1 products, the science team recommends using a combination of the existing QC bits, emissivity values, and estimated product errors, to confidently remove bad pixels from analysis. For more details, refer to this dust and cloud contamination [example documentation](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/data/userguide/MOD21_dust_QC_examples.pdf). * For complete information about known issues please refer to the [MODIS/VIIRS Land Quality Assessment website](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/knownissue?sensor=MODIS&sat=Terra&as=61).

evidence: NASA CMR concept C2565791050-LPCLOUD returned short_name MOD21C3, version 061, title 'MODIS/Terra Land Surface Temperature/3-Band Emissivity Monthly L3 Global 0.05Deg CMG V061', data_center LPCLOUD, DOI https://doi.org/10.5067/MODIS/MOD21C3.061, Earthdata catalog URL https://www.earthdata.nasa.gov/data/catalog/lpcloud-mod21c3-061, Earthdata Search URL https://search.earthdata.nasa.gov/search/granules?p=C2565791050-LPCLOUD, and summary: A suite of Moderate Resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer (MODIS) Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity (LST&E) products are available in Collection 6.1. The MOD21 Land Surface Temperature (LST) algorithm differs from the algorithm of the [MOD11](https://doi.org/10.5067/modis/mod11_l2.061) LST products, in that the MOD21 algorithm is based on the ASTER Temperature/Emissivity Separation (TES) technique, whereas the MOD11 uses the split-window technique. The MOD21 TES algorithm uses a physics-based algorithm to dynamically retrieve both the LST and spectral emissivity simultaneously from the MODIS thermal infrared bands 29, 31, and 32. The TES algorithm is combined with an improved Water Vapor Scaling (WVS) atmospheric correction scheme to stabilize the retrieval during very warm and humid conditions. The MOD21C3 dataset is a monthly composite LST product that uses an algorithm based on a simple averaging method. The algorithm calculates the average from all the cloud free [MOD21A1D](http://doi.org/10.5067/MODIS/MOD21A1D.061) and [MOD21A1N](http://doi.org/10.5067/MODIS/MOD21A1N.061) daily acquisitions from the 8-day period. Unlike the MOD21A1 data sets where the daytime and nighttime acquisitions are separate products, the MOD21A2 contains both daytime and nighttime acquisitions as separate Science Dataset (SDS) layers within a single Hierarchical Data Format (HDF) file. The LST, Quality Control (QC), view zenith angle, and viewing time have separate day and night SDS layers, while the values for the MODIS emissivity bands 29, 31, and 32 are the average of both the nighttime and daytime acquisitions. Additional details regarding the method used to create this Level 3 (L3) product are available in the Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document (ATBD). Known Issues * Users of MODIS LST products may notice an increase in occurrences of [extreme high temperature outliers](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/displayissue?id=117) in the unfiltered MxD21 Version 6 and 6.1 products compared to the heritage MxD11 LST products. This can occur especially over desert regions like the Sahara where undetected cloud and dust can negatively impact both the MxD21 and MxD11 retrieval algorithms. * In the MxD11 LST products, these contaminated pixels are flagged in the algorithm and set to fill values in the output products based on differences in the band 32 and band 31 radiances used in the generalized split window algorithm. In the MxD21 LST products, values for the contaminated pixels are retained in the output products (and may result in overestimated temperatures), and users need to apply Quality Control (QC) filtering and other error analyses for filtering out bad values. High temperature outlier thresholds are not employed in MxD21 since it would potentially remove naturally occurring hot surface targets such as fires and lava flows. * High atmospheric aerosol optical depth (AOD) caused by vast dust outbreaks in the Sahara and other deserts highlighted in the example documentation are the primary reason for high outlier surface temperature values (and corresponding low emissivity values) in the MxD21 LST products. Future versions of the MxD21 product will include a dust flag from the MODIS aerosol product and/or brightness temperature look up tables to filter out contaminated dust pixels. It should be noted that in the MxD11B day/night algorithm products, more advanced cloud filtering is employed in the multi-day products based on a temporal analysis of historical LST over cloudy areas. This may result in more stringent filtering of dust contaminated pixels in these products. * In order to mitigate the impact of dust in the MxD21 V6 and 6.1 products, the science team recommends using a combination of the existing QC bits, emissivity values, and estimated product errors, to confidently remove bad pixels from analysis. For more details, refer to this dust and cloud contamination [example documentation](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/data/userguide/MOD21_dust_QC_examples.pdf). * For complete information about known issues please refer to the [MODIS/VIIRS Land Quality Assessment website](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/knownissue?sensor=MODIS&sat=Terra&as=61).
scale: monthly 0.05 degree CMG composite

A suite of MODIS Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity (LST&E) products are available in Collection 6.1. The MYD21 LST algorithm differs from the algorithm of the [MYD11](https://doi.org/10.5067/modis/myd11_l2.061) LST products, in that the MYD21 algorithm is based on the ASTER Temperature/Emissivity Separation (TES) technique, whereas the MYD11 uses the split-window technique. The TES algorithm is combined with an improved Water Vapor Scaling (WVS) atmospheric correction scheme to stabilize the retrieval during very warm and humid conditions. The MYD21A1D dataset is produced daily from daytime Level 2 Gridded (L2G) intermediate LST products. The L2G process maps the daily [MYD21](http://doi.org/10.5067/MODIS/MYD21.061) swath granules onto a sinusoidal MODIS grid and stores all observations falling over a gridded cell for a given day. The MOD21A1 algorithm sorts through all these observations for each cell and estimates the final LST value as an average from all observations that are cloud free and have good LST&E accuracies. time_span: See CMR collection metadata for temporal extent
notes: Accepted from NASA CMR official collection metadata in v112. MOD11 is the heritage split-window LST&E family, distinct from MOD21 TES rows. The MOD11_L2 Version 6.1 swath product provides per-pixel Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity (LST&E) with a pixel size of 1,000 meters (m). The product is produced daily in 5-minute temporal increments of satellite acquisition using the generalized split-window algorithm. MOD11_L2 is a Level 2 product which provides the input for the Level 3 products. Provided in each MOD11_L2 file are LST, quality control assessment, error estimates, bands 31 and 32 emissivities, zenith angle of the pixel view, observation time, and the geographic coordinates for every five scan lines and samples. Known Issues * For complete information about known issues please refer to the [MODIS/VIIRS Land Quality Assessment website](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/knownissue?sensor=MODIS&sat=Terra&as=61).

notes: Accepted from NASA CMR official collection metadata in v114 as a VIIRS standard archive active-fire / thermal-anomaly product. This row is distinct from the v001 FIRMS aggregate service and from v002 NRT rows because it represents the standard archive product with its own DOI and CMR concept. The Visible Infrared Imaging Radiometer Suite (VIIRS) Active Fires (VNP14IMG) Version 2 product is produced in 6-minute temporal satellite increments (swaths) at 375 meter resolution from the VIIRS sensor aboard the NASA/NOAA Suomi National Polar-orbiting Partnership (Suomi NPP) satellite. This Level 2 product is designed after the Terra and Aqua Moderate Resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer (MODIS) Thermal Anomalies and Fire data products to promote the continuity of the Earth Observation System (EOS) mission. This data product can enable users to understand the location and intensity of fire events. Due to its higher spatial resolution, the VNP14IMG active fire product provides greater response over fires of relatively small areas, as well as improved mapping of large fire perimeters in comparison to the VNP14 fire data product. The VNP14IMG product includes 25 science dataset variables to analyze key factors in fire detection, including atmospheric conditions (e.g., radiance, solar zenith angle, brightness temperature) and fuel type for the event. The fire mask variable in the VNP14IMG product is the primary variable and can be used to identify fires and other thermal anomalies such as volcanoes. Each swath of data is approximately 3,060 kilometers along track (long) and 3,060 kilometers across track (wide). The VNP14IMG product is also used to generate higher-level fire data products. Use of the (VNP03MODLL) data product is required to apply accurate geolocation information to the VNP14IMG Science Datasets (SDS) / Variables. Known Issues * For complete information about known issues please refer to the [MODIS/VIIRS Land Quality Assessment website](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/knownissue?sensor=VIIRS).

The daily NASA/NOAA Suomi National Polar-orbiting Partnership (Suomi NPP) Visible Infrared Imaging Radiometer Suite (VIIRS) Thermal Anomalies and Fire (VNP14A1) Version 2 data product provides daily information about active fires and other thermal anomalies. The VNP14A1 data product is a global, 1 kilometer (km) gridded composite of fire pixels detected from VIIRS 750 meter (m) bands over a daily (24-hour) period. The VNP14 data products are designed after the Moderate Resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer (MODIS) Thermal Anomalies/Fire product suite. The VNP14A1 product provides a total of four Science Dataset (SDS) layers for the confidence of fire, maximum fire radiative power (FRP), quality assessment (QA), and position of fire within scan. Each data product file is provided in HDF-EOS5 format. A low resolution browse is also provided showing the fire mask layer with a color map applied in JPEG format. Known Issues * For complete information about known issues please refer to the [MODIS/VIIRS Land Quality Assessment website](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/knownissue?sensor=VIIRS).

Each data product file is provided in HDF-EOS5 format. A low resolution browse is also provided showing the fire mask variable with a color map applied in JPEG format. Known Issues * For complete information about known issues please refer to the [MODIS/VIIRS Land Quality Assessment website](https://landweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/knownissue?sensor=VIIRS&sat=J2).

desc_en: NASA VIIRS/JPSS2 Thermal Anomalies and Fire Daily L3 Global 1km SIN Grid V002 is a VIIRS standard-archive active-fire and thermal-anomaly product. The daily Level 3 1 km SIN Grid product organizes NOAA-21/JPSS-2 VIIRS fire and thermal-anomaly information into global grids, completing the JPSS2 daily fire product entry. It directly supports infrared remote-sensing applications for wildfire, volcanic thermal anomalies, industrial high-temperature sources and long-term fire statistics, complementing aggregate FIRMS services and near-real-time NRT streams.

desc_zh: FY-4A AGRI Fire and Hot Spot Detection China Regional L2 FHS 是国家卫星气象中心发布的风云四号 A 星 AGRI 二级火点/热源点检测产品。NSMC 元数据说明该产品利用中红外通道对高温热源敏感的特性，并结合多通道对云、水体、植被等目标的光谱差异，消除太阳辐射干扰，提取陆地火点信息并估算亚像元火点面积和温度。产品为中国区域标称投影 NetCDF，空间分辨率 2 km，内容包括火点像元位置、亚像元面积、火点强度、行政区划、土地利用类型和观测时间等。
desc_en: FY-4A AGRI Fire and Hot Spot Detection China Regional L2 FHS is a Fengyun-4A AGRI Level-2 fire and hot-spot detection product from the National Satellite Meteorological Center. NSMC metadata says it uses the mid-infrared channel sensitivity to high-temperature hot spots together with multi-channel spectral characteristics of clouds, water and vegetation to reduce solar-radiation interference, extract land fire information and estimate sub-pixel fire area and temperature. The China regional nominal-projection NetCDF product has 2 km spatial resolution and includes fire-pixel location, sub-pixel area, fire intensity, administrative region, land-use type and observation time.

openness: open institutional metadata and Fengyun DataPortal access under NSMC/CMA provider terms; registration may be required for product download

evidence: NSMC DataPortal product-info API for fileNamePromise FY4A-_AGRI--_N_REGC_1047E_L2-_FHS-_MULT_NOM_YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_yyyymmddhhmmss_2000M_V0001.NC returned productionnameeng 'AGRI Fire and Hot Spot detection (China Regional)', satellite FY4A, instrument AGRI, datatype FHS, level L2, area REGC, channel MULT, projection NOM, resolution 2000M, format NC, MOID 1.2.156.416.CMA.D3.NSMC.DAT.SIC.20190430.AF2BFC2F.000A, data begin 2019-01-18, data end 2024-03-04, file count 388156, total size 167133976546 bytes, and updated timestamp 2026-06-03 14:01:02. English introduction: Fire/Hot spot detection product (FHS) refer to the fire point distribution, fire point intensity and fire point thematic map products generated by Fengyun-4A (FY-4A) multi-channel scanning imaging radiometer data. The product is generated based on the sensitivity of mid-infrared channel to high-temperature hot spot and the spectral characteristics of cloud area, water and vegetation in different channels. By eliminating the interference of solar radiation, the fire point information is extracted from the land area within the observation range of Fengyun-4A, and the area and temperature of the sub-pixel fire point are further estimated. The spatial resolution of the product are 2 kilometers. English purpose: The product includes the location of fire point pixels, sub-pixel fire point area, fire point intensity, administrative division and land use type of fire point, observation time, etc., which will provide large-scale continuous dynamic monitoring information of fire field for forest, grassland fire prevention and forest grassland fire risk weather forecasting, and provide biomass combustion (including forest grassland fire, straw burning, etc.) fire information in Asia for climate research and ecological environmental protection departments.

The CSR product is a subset of the information derived during the Scenes Analysis processing. The product provides the brightness temperature for a subset of the MSG channels averaged over all pixels within a processing segment (16x16 pixels) which have been identified as clear (a minimum of 7 clear pixels are needed to compute the average). For the channel WV6.2 the CSR is also derived for areas containing low-level clouds. The CSR product also contains the fraction of clear sky and the solar zenith angle. The final CSR product is BUFR encoded 3-hourly at every third quarter of the hour (e.g. 00:45, 01:45 ...). Note that the reprocessing was done using the latest version of the EUMETSAT software (Version 1.5.3, 2013) ingesting  original level 1.5 SEVIRI images and the ECMWF ERA-interim as a  as a fore. desc_zh: EUMETSAT IASI Level 1C Climate Data Record Release 1 - Metop-A 是 Metop-A IASI 红外大气探测干涉仪的基础气候数据记录，覆盖 2007 年 7 月至 2017 年 12 月。它提供 0.25 cm-1 分辨率的红外辐射谱，每个探测像元含 8461 个谱样本，可用于温湿廓线、臭氧等痕量气体、陆海表面温度、发射率和云性质研究；2017 年数据存在官方 erratum，使用时应参考操作型 IASI L1C 替代建议。
desc_en: EUMETSAT IASI Level 1C Climate Data Record Release 1 - Metop-A is the Fundamental Climate Data Record for the Metop-A Infrared Atmospheric Sounding Interferometer, spanning July 2007 through December 2017. It provides infrared radiance spectra at 0.25 cm-1 resolution with 8461 spectral samples per sounded pixel for temperature and humidity profiles, trace gases such as ozone, surface temperature, emissivity and cloud-property studies; the metadata carries an erratum for 2017 data and recommends operational IASI L1C from February 2017 onward.

coverage: MTG 0 degree service area; geostationary FCI IR blended with LEO microwave precipitation and gauge adjustment according to API abstract
license: EUMETSAT browse API rights: NoConditions; Data Store terms apply
formats: EUMETSAT Data Store product family; accumulated precipitation fields for 3, 6, 12 and 24 h according to API abstract
access: EUMETSAT Data Store map/download entry; EUMETSAT browse API collection metadata; User Portal catalogue page
doi_or_citation: EO:EUM:DAT:1087
time_span: 2025-08-14/ according to EUMETSAT browse API collection metadata
notes: Accepted as directly infrared-related because the API abstract identifies blended MW+IR and FCI input. Distinct from v124 row EO:EUM:DAT:1086, which is the instantaneous precipitation rate product, while this row is accumulated precipitation. API abstract: Derived from precipitation maps generated by merging MW images from operational sun-synchronous satellites and IR images from geostationary satellites.  Integration is performed over 3, 6, 12 and 24 h. In order to reduce biases, the satellite-derived field is forced to match raingauge observations and, in future, the accumulated precipitation field outputted from a NWP model
desc_zh: EUMETSAT MTG Accumulated precipitation at ground by blended MW+IR and FCI 是 MTG 0 度服务区的地面累积降水产品。它由业务太阳同步卫星微波图像与静止卫星红外图像融合生成，并提供 3、6、12、24 小时累积场，后续通过雨量计观测约束以降低偏差，适合快速降水监测和水文气象应用。
desc_en: EUMETSAT MTG Accumulated precipitation at ground by blended MW+IR and FCI is an accumulated precipitation product for the MTG 0-degree service area. It merges microwave imagery from operational sun-synchronous satellites with geostationary infrared imagery and provides 3, 6, 12 and 24 hour accumulation fields, with gauge forcing to reduce bias for rapid precipitation monitoring and hydrometeorological applications.

provider: EUMETSAT / Meteosat Third Generation FCI cloud products

coverage: MTG 0 degree full disk / FCI cloud-mask coverage

doi_or_citation: EO:EUM:DAT:0800
time_span: 2025-01-24/ according to EUMETSAT browse API collection metadata
notes: Accepted as a GRIB2-format/collection entry that supports infrared downstream retrievals. v124 contains broader MTG FCI cloud products, but no EO:EUM:DAT:0800 collection row. API abstract: The central aim of the cloud mask (CLM) product is to identify cloudy and cloud free FCI Level 1c pixels with high confidence. The product also provides information on the presence of snow/sea ice, volcanic ash and dust. This information is crucial both for spatiotemporal analyses of the cloud coverage and for the subsequent retrieval of other meteorological products that are only valid for cloudy (eg, cloud properties) or clear pixels (eg, clear sky reflectance maps or global instability indices). The algorithm is based on multispectral threshold techniques applied to each pixel of the image. CLM is available in netCDF and GRIB format, every 10 minutes, at a spatial resolution of 2 km at nadir.
desc_zh: EUMETSAT MTG Cloud Mask GRIB2 产品用于高置信度识别 FCI 1c 像元的有云与无云状态，并提供雪/海冰、火山灰和沙尘等信息。它是云覆盖时空分析以及后续气象产品反演的重要输入，对红外云顶、辐射和地表温度类产品的质量控制具有直接支撑作用。
desc_en: EUMETSAT MTG Cloud Mask GRIB2 identifies cloudy and cloud-free FCI Level 1c pixels with high confidence and also reports snow or sea ice, volcanic ash and dust information. It is a core input for spatiotemporal cloud-cover analysis and downstream meteorological retrievals, directly supporting quality control for infrared cloud-top, radiance and land-surface-temperature products.
